================================Statement Details================================

Statement Coverage for instance /tb_mipi_system_loopback/dut/u_csi2_tx_top/u_regfile --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File tx_regfile.v
    23                                               module apb_csi2_tx_regfile #(
    24                                                   parameter integer APB_ADDR_WIDTH = 32
    25                                               ) (
    26                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    27                                                   // APB Clock and Reset
    28                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    29                                                   input  wire                      PCLK,       // APB clock
    30                                                   input  wire                      PRESETn,    // APB reset, active-LOW
    31                                               
    32                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    33                                                   // APB Slave Interface
    34                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    35                                                   input  wire [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] PADDR,      // Byte address
    36                                                   input  wire                      PSEL,       // Peripheral select
    37                                                   input  wire                      PENABLE,    // ACCESS phase strobe
    38                                                   input  wire                      PWRITE,     // 1=Write 0=Read
    39                                                   input  wire [31:0]               PWDATA,     // Write data (32-bit bus)
    40                                                   input  wire [3:0]                PSTRB,      // Write byte strobes
    41                                               
    42                                                   output reg  [31:0]               PRDATA,     // Read data
    43                                                   output wire                      PREADY,     // Slave ready (no wait states)
    44                                                   output wire                      PSLVERR,    // Slave error
    45                                               
    46                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    47                                                   // Register outputs to CSI-2 TX Core
    48                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    49                                                   output reg                       tx_en,      // TX_CTRL[0] - TX Enable
    50                                                   output reg                       sw_rst,     // TX_CTRL[1] - SW Reset pulse
    51                                                   output reg                       scram_enb,  // TX_CFG[0]  - Scramble Enable
    52                                                   output reg                       crc_enb     // TX_CFG[1]  - CRC Enable
    53                                               );
    54                                               
    55                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    56                                                   // Local Parameters - Register Addresses
    57                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    58                                                   localparam [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] ADDR_TX_CTRL = 'h000;
    59                                                   localparam [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] ADDR_TX_CFG  = 'h004;
    60                                               
    61                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    62                                                   // Internal Wires - APB Phase & Address Decode
    63                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    64                                                   wire wr_en;
    65                                                   wire rd_en;
    66                                                   wire sel_tx_ctrl;
    67                                                   wire sel_tx_cfg;
    68                                                   wire addr_valid;
    69                                               
    70              1                        488         assign wr_en = PSEL & PENABLE &  PWRITE;
    71              1                        488         assign rd_en = PSEL & PENABLE & ~PWRITE;
    72                                               
    73              1                        110         assign sel_tx_ctrl = (PADDR[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2] == ADDR_TX_CTRL[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2]);
    74              1                        110         assign sel_tx_cfg  = (PADDR[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2] == ADDR_TX_CFG [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2]);
    75              1                        110         assign addr_valid  = sel_tx_ctrl | sel_tx_cfg;
    76                                               
    77                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    78                                                   // APB Response Signals
    79                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    80                                                   assign PREADY  = 1'b1;
    81              1                        489         assign PSLVERR = PSEL & PENABLE & ~addr_valid;
    82                                               
    83                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    84                                                   // Register Write Logic
    85                                                   //
    86                                                   // Priority (highest to lowest):
    87                                                   //   1. PRESETn LOW      - async reset clears all registers
    88                                                   //   2. SW_RST self-clear - takes priority over APB write in same cycle
    89                                                   //   3. APB write        - only fires when SW_RST is not self-clearing
    90                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    91              1                        705         always @(posedge PCLK or negedge PRESETn) begin
    92                                                       if (!PRESETn) begin
    93              1                        110                 tx_en     <= 1'b0;
    94              1                        110                 sw_rst    <= 1'b0;
    95              1                        110                 scram_enb <= 1'b0;
    96              1                        110                 crc_enb   <= 1'b0;
    97                                               
    98                                                       end else if (sw_rst) begin
    99              1                         54                 sw_rst <= 1'b0;
    100                                              
    101                                                      end else if (wr_en) begin
    102                                                          //----------------------------------------------------------------
    103                                                          // APB Write
    104                                                          //----------------------------------------------------------------
    105                                              
    106                                                          // TX_CTRL @ 0x000
    107                                                          if (sel_tx_ctrl && PSTRB[0]) begin
    108             1                        108                     tx_en  <= PWDATA[0];    // [0] TX_EN
    109             1                        108                     sw_rst <= PWDATA[1];    // [1] SW_RST
    110                                                              // [31:2] reserved
    111                                                          end
    112                                              
    113                                                          // TX_CFG @ 0x004
    114                                                          if (sel_tx_cfg && PSTRB[0]) begin
    115             1                         54                     scram_enb <= PWDATA[0]; // [0] SCRAM_ENB
    116             1                         54                     crc_enb   <= PWDATA[1]; // [1] CRC_ENB
    117                                                              // [31:2] reserved
    118                                                          end
    119                                              
    120                                                      end
    121                                                  end
    122                                              
    123                                                  //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    124                                                  // APB Read
    125                                                  //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    126             1                        369         always @(*) begin
    127             1                        369             PRDATA = 32'h0000_0000;
    128                                              
    129                                                      if (rd_en) begin
    130                                                          case (1'b1)
    131             1                    ***0***                     sel_tx_ctrl : PRDATA = {{30{1'b0}}, sw_rst,   tx_en    };
    132             1                    ***0***                     sel_tx_cfg  : PRDATA = {{30{1'b0}}, crc_enb,  scram_enb};
    133             1                    ***0***                     default     : PRDATA = 32'h0000_0000;

    9

    23                                                   output reg         o_byte_valid,
    24                                                   output reg         o_byte_frame_start,
    25                                                   output reg         o_byte_frame_end,
    26                                                   output reg         o_byte_line_start,
    27                                                   output reg         o_byte_packet_done
    28                                               );
    29                                               
    30                                                   // =========================================================================
    31                                                   // 1. Parameters & Configuration Decoding
    32                                                   // =========================================================================
    33                                                   localparam DT_RAW8   = 6'h2A;
    34                                                   localparam DT_YUV422 = 6'h1E;
    35                                                   localparam DT_RGB565 = 6'h22;
    36                                                   localparam DT_RGB888 = 6'h24;
    37                                                   localparam DT_RAW10  = 6'h2B;
    38                                               
    39                                                   reg [2:0]  cfg_byte_ratio; 
    40                                                   reg        cfg_mode_gearbox;
    41                                                   reg        cfg_is_rgb565_yuv;
    42                                               
    43              1                         82         always @(*) begin
    44              1                         82             cfg_is_rgb565_yuv = 1'b0;
    45                                                       case (i_cfg_data_type)
    46                                                           DT_RAW8: begin
    47              1                         41                     cfg_byte_ratio   = 3'd1;
    48              1                         41                     cfg_mode_gearbox = 1'b0;
    49                                                           end
    50                                                           DT_YUV422, DT_RGB565: begin
    51              1                         20                     cfg_byte_ratio   = 3'd2;
    52              1                         20                     cfg_mode_gearbox = 1'b0;
    53              1                         20                     cfg_is_rgb565_yuv = 1'b1;
    54                                                           end
    55                                                           DT_RGB888: begin
    56              1                         10                     cfg_byte_ratio   = 3'd3;
    57              1                         10                     cfg_mode_gearbox = 1'b0;
    58                                                           end
    59                                                           DT_RAW10: begin
    60              1                         11                     cfg_byte_ratio   = 3'd5; 
    61              1                         11                     cfg_mode_gearbox = 1'b1;
    62                                                           end
    63                                                           default: begin 
    64              1                    ***0***                     cfg_byte_ratio   = 3'd1;
    65              1                    ***0***                     cfg_mode_gearbox = 1'b0;
    66                                                           end
    67                                                       endcase
    68                                                   end
    69                                               
    70                                                   // =========================================================================
    71                                                   // 2. FSM Next-State Logic (STARC05-2.11.3.1 Compliance)
    72                                                   // =========================================================================
    73                                                   reg [2:0]  state_cnt;
    74                                                   reg [2:0]  next_state_cnt;
    75                                                   
    76                                                   reg [23:0] pixel_shift_reg;
    77                                                   reg [7:0]  lsb_buffer;
    78                                                   reg        active_eol_pending;
    79                                                   reg        next_pixel_is_line_start;
    80                                                   reg [15:0] line_count; 
    81                                                   reg [7:0]  gap_cnt; 
    82                                               
    83                                                   // Combinational block strictly for FSM next state
    84              1                    8437108         always @(*) begin
    85              1                    8437108             next_state_cnt = state_cnt; // Default: hold state
    86                                               
    87                                                       if (gap_cnt == 0) begin
    88                                                           if (!cfg_mode_gearbox) begin
    89                                                               if (state_cnt == 0) begin
    90                                                                   if (i_native_valid && i_cfg_data_type != DT_RAW8) begin
    91              1                    3000000                             next_state_cnt = state_cnt + 1'b1;
    92                                                                   end
    93                                                               end else begin
    94                                                                   if (state_cnt == (cfg_byte_ratio - 1)) begin
    95              1                    3000000                             next_state_cnt = 3'd0;
    96                                                                   end else begin
    97              1                    1000000                             next_state_cnt = state_cnt + 1'b1;
    98                                                                   end
    99                                                               end
    100                                                          end else begin
    101                                                              // Gearbox mode
    102                                                              if (state_cnt < 4) begin
    103                                                                  if (i_native_valid) begin
    104                                                                      if (state_cnt == 3'd3 || i_native_eol) begin
    105             1                     275000                                 next_state_cnt = 3'd4;
    106                                                                      end else begin
    107             1                     825000                                 next_state_cnt = state_cnt + 1'b1;
    108                                                                      end
    109                                                                  end
    110                                                              end else if (state_cnt == 4) begin
    111             1                     275000                         next_state_cnt = 3'd0;
    112                                                              end
    113                                                          end
    114                                                      end
    115                                                  end
    116                                              
    117                                                  // =========================================================================
    118                                                  // 3. Main Sequential Logic
    119                                                  // =========================================================================
    120             1                    9736736         always @(posedge i_byte_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin
    121                                                      if (!i_rst_n) begin
    122             1                        146                 state_cnt                <= 3'd0;
    123             1                        146                 o_byte_valid             <= 1'b0;
    124             1                        146                 o_byte_data              <= 8'd0;
    125             1                        146                 o_native_ready           <= 1'b1; 
    126             1                        146                 o_byte_frame_start       <= 1'b0;
    127             1                        146                 o_byte_frame_end         <= 1'b0;
    128             1                        146                 o_byte_line_start        <= 1'b0;
    129             1                        146                 o_byte_packet_done       <= 1'b0;
    130             1                        146                 lsb_buffer               <= 8'd0;
    131             1                        146                 pixel_shift_reg          <= 24'd0;
    132             1                        146                 active_eol_pending       <= 1'b0;
    133             1                        146                 next_pixel_is_line_start <= 1'b1;
    134             1                        146                 line_count               <= 16'd0;
    135             1                        146                 gap_cnt                  <= 8'd0; 
    136                                                      end else begin
    137                                                          // Default clears
    138             1                    9736590                 o_byte_frame_start <= 1'b0;
    139             1                    9736590                 o_byte_frame_end   <= 1'b0;
    140             1                    9736590                 o_byte_line_start  <= 1'b0;
    141             1                    9736590                 o_byte_packet_done <= 1'b0;
    142             1                    9736590                 o_byte_valid       <= 1'b0;
    143                                                          
    144                                                          // FSM State Update (Separated from Next State Logic)
    145             1                    9736590                 state_cnt <= next_state_cnt;
    146                                              
    147                                                          if (gap_cnt > 0) begin
    148             1                      54020                     gap_cnt <= gap_cnt - 1'b1;
    149             1                       5402                     if (gap_cnt == 8'd1) o_native_ready <= 1'b1; 
    150             1                      48618                     else                 o_native_ready <= 1'b0; 
    151                                                          end 
    152                                                          else begin
    153                                                              // -----------------------------------------------------------------
    154                                                              // MODE A: Simple Serialization (RAW8, RGB565, RGB888, YUV422)
    155                                                              // -----------------------------------------------------------------
    156                                                              if (!cfg_mode_gearbox) begin
    157                                                                  if (state_cnt == 0) begin
    158                                                                      if (i_native_valid) begin
    159             1                    4302000                                 o_native_ready <= 1'b0;
    160             1                    4302000                                 o_byte_valid <= 1'b1;
    161             1                    4302000                                 active_eol_pending <= i_native_eol;
    162                                              
    163                                                                          // Frame/Line Start (STARC05-2.2.3.3 Fix: Merged double assignments)
    164                                                                          if (i_native_sof) begin
    165             1                         45                                     o_byte_frame_start       <= 1'b1;
    166             1                         45                                     o_byte_line_start        <= 1'b1;
    167             1                         45                                     line_count               <= 16'd1;
    168             1                         45                                     next_pixel_is_line_start <= (i_cfg_data_type == DT_RAW8 && i_native_eol) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
    169                                                                          end else if (next_pixel_is_line_start) begin
    170             1                       4257                                     o_byte_line_start        <= 1'b1;
    171             1                       4257                                     line_count               <= line_count + 16'd1;
    172             1                       4257                                     next_pixel_is_line_start <= (i_cfg_data_type == DT_RAW8 && i_native_eol) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
    173                                                                          end else if (i_cfg_data_type == DT_RAW8 && i_native_eol) begin
    174             1                       1302                                     next_pixel_is_line_start <= 1'b1;
    175                                                                          end
    176                                              
    177                                                                          if (i_cfg_data_type == DT_RAW8) begin
    178             1                    1302000                                     o_byte_data <= i_native_data[7:0];
    179                                                                              if (i_native_eol) begin
    180             1                       1302                                         o_byte_packet_done <= 1'b1;
    181                                                                                  if (line_count == i_frame_num_lines)
    182             1                         13                                             o_byte_frame_end <= 1'b1;
    183                                                                                      
    184                                                                                  if (INTER_PACKET_GAP > 0) begin
    185             1                       1302                                             gap_cnt <= INTER_PACKET_GAP;
    186             1                       1302                                             o_native_ready <= 1'b0;
    187                                                                                  end else o_native_ready <= 1'b1;

    190                                                                              end

    199                                                                          end
    200                                                                      end else begin
    201             1                       4448                                 o_native_ready <= 1'b1;
    202                                                                      end
    203                                                                  end else begin

    57                                                   // =========================================================================

    59                                                   // Description: Tracks the current target lane for the incoming byte.

    102                                                  // 3. Valid Generation & Flush Controller

    18                                               module async_fifo_ppi_tx #(  
    19                                                   parameter WIDTH = 8,
    20                                                   parameter DEPTH = 16
    21                                               )(
    22                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23                                                   // Write Domain Interface (Source)
    24                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    25                                                   input  wire              i_wr_clk,
    26                                                   input  wire              i_wr_rst_n, 
    27                                                   input  wire              i_wr_en,
    28                                                   input  wire [WIDTH-1:0]  i_din,
    29                                                   output wire              o_full,
    30                                               
    31                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32                                                   // Read Domain Interface (Destination)
    33                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34                                                   input  wire              i_rd_clk,
    35                                                   input  wire              i_rd_rst_n, 
    36                                                   input  wire              i_rd_en,
    37                                                   output wire [WIDTH-1:0]  o_dout,
    38                                                   output wire              o_empty
    39                                               );
    40                                               
    41                                                   // =========================================================================
    42                                                   // Local Parameters & Internal Signals
    43                                                   // =========================================================================
    44                                                   // Address width calculation based on parameterized FIFO depth
    45                                                   localparam ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH);
    46                                               
    47                                                   // Internal Memory Array (Dual-Port RAM configuration)
    48                                                   reg [WIDTH-1:0] mem [DEPTH-1:0];
    49                                               
    50                                                   // Pointers: ADDR_WIDTH+1 is used for Full/Empty wrap-around detection
    51                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr, rd_ptr;
    52                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray, rd_ptr_gray; 
    53                                                   
    54                                                   // Synchronizer Flip-Flops (2-stage synchronizers for CDC safety)
    55                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray_sync1, wr_ptr_gray_sync2;
    56                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_gray_sync1, rd_ptr_gray_sync2;
    57                                               
    58                                                   // Combinational next-state pointers for faster evaluation
    59              1                    9704881         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_next = wr_ptr + 1;
    60              1                    9704881         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_next = rd_ptr + 1;
    61                                                   
    62                                                   // Loop variable for DFT-compliant memory reset
    63                                                   integer j;
    64                                               
    65                                                   // =========================================================================
    66                                                   // 1. Write Domain Logic
    67                                                   // Description: Handles pointer incrementing and Binary-to-Gray conversion.
    68                                                   //              Binary pointers are used for memory addressing, while Gray 
    69                                                   //              pointers are used for safe clock domain crossing.
    70                                                   // =========================================================================
    71              1                   16628441         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    72                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    73              1                        218                 wr_ptr <= 0;
    74              1                        218                 wr_ptr_gray <= 0; 
    75                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    76              1                    9704852                 wr_ptr <= wr_ptr_next;
    77                                                           // Binary to Gray conversion: XOR shifted value with original
    78              1                    9704852                 wr_ptr_gray <= wr_ptr_next ^ (wr_ptr_next >> 1); 
    79                                                       end
    80                                                   end
    81                                               
    82                                                   // =========================================================================
    83                                                   // 2. Memory Write Operation (DFT-Compliant)
    84                                                   // Description: Writes data to the memory array. Incorporates a full reset 
    85                                                   //              loop to synthesize into standard D-Flip-Flops with reset 
    86                                                   //              pins, ensuring compatibility with DFT scan chains.

    90              2                       3488     
    91              1                       3488                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    92                                                           end

    94              1                    9704852                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    95                                                       end
    96                                                   end
    97

    99                                                   // 3. Read Domain Logic
    100                                                  // Description: Manages the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    101                                                  // =========================================================================
    102             1                   20772338         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    103                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin

    105             1                        108                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;

    108                                                          // Binary to Gray conversion
    109             1                    9704852                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    110                                                      end
    111                                                  end
    112                                                  
    113                                                  // Continuous assignment for data output, gated by the 'o_empty' flag
    114                                                  // to prevent reading garbage/stale data.
    115             1                   22167711         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    116                                              
    117                                                  // =========================================================================
    118                                                  // 4. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    119                                                  // Description: Synchronizes Gray-coded pointers into the opposite domain
    120                                                  //              using standard 2-FF synchronizers to mitigate metastability.
    121                                                  // =========================================================================
    122                                                  
    123                                                  // Synchronize Read Pointer into Write Clock Domain
    124             1                   20772503         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    125                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    126             1                        218                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    127             1                        218                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    128                                                      end else begin
    129             1                   20772285                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    130             1                   20772285                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    131                                                      end
    132                                                  end
    133

    135             1                   20772324         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    136                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    137             1                        123                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    138             1                        123                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    139                                                      end else begin
    140             1                   20772201                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    141             1                   20772201                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    142                                                      end
    143                                                  end
    144                                              
    145                                                  // =========================================================================
    146                                                  // 5. Status Flag Generation
    147                                                  // Description: 
    148                                                  //   - o_full : True if write pointer matches synchronized read pointer, 
    149                                                  //              accounting for the wrap-around bit in the Gray domain.
    150                                                  //   - o_empty: True if read pointer matches synchronized write pointer.

Statement Coverage for instance /tb_mipi_system_loopback/dut/u_csi2_tx_top/u_tx/fifo2 --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source

  File async_fifo_ppi_tx.v
    18                                               module async_fifo_ppi_tx #(  
    19                                                   parameter WIDTH = 8,
    20                                                   parameter DEPTH = 16
    21                                               )(
    22                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23                                                   // Write Domain Interface (Source)
    24                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    25                                                   input  wire              i_wr_clk,
    26                                                   input  wire              i_wr_rst_n, 
    27                                                   input  wire              i_wr_en,
    28                                                   input  wire [WIDTH-1:0]  i_din,
    29                                                   output wire              o_full,
    30                                               
    31                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32                                                   // Read Domain Interface (Destination)
    33                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34                                                   input  wire              i_rd_clk,
    35                                                   input  wire              i_rd_rst_n, 
    36                                                   input  wire              i_rd_en,
    37                                                   output wire [WIDTH-1:0]  o_dout,
    38                                                   output wire              o_empty
    39                                               );
    40                                               
    41                                                   // =========================================================================
    42                                                   // Local Parameters & Internal Signals
    43                                                   // =========================================================================
    44                                                   // Address width calculation based on parameterized FIFO depth
    45                                                   localparam ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH);
    46                                               
    47                                                   // Internal Memory Array (Dual-Port RAM configuration)
    48                                                   reg [WIDTH-1:0] mem [DEPTH-1:0];
    49                                               
    50                                                   // Pointers: ADDR_WIDTH+1 is used for Full/Empty wrap-around detection
    51                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr, rd_ptr;
    52                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray, rd_ptr_gray; 
    53                                                   
    54                                                   // Synchronizer Flip-Flops (2-stage synchronizers for CDC safety)
    55                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray_sync1, wr_ptr_gray_sync2;
    56                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_gray_sync1, rd_ptr_gray_sync2;
    57                                               
    58                                                   // Combinational next-state pointers for faster evaluation
    59              1                    4139626         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_next = wr_ptr + 1;
    60              1                    4139626         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_next = rd_ptr + 1;
    61                                                   
    62                                                   // Loop variable for DFT-compliant memory reset
    63                                                   integer j;
    64                                               
    65                                                   // =========================================================================
    66                                                   // 1. Write Domain Logic
    67                                                   // Description: Handles pointer incrementing and Binary-to-Gray conversion.
    68                                                   //              Binary pointers are used for memory addressing, while Gray 
    69                                                   //              pointers are used for safe clock domain crossing.
    70                                                   // =========================================================================
    71              1                    8279551         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    72                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    73              1                        218                 wr_ptr <= 0;
    74              1                        218                 wr_ptr_gray <= 0; 
    75                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    76              1                    4139612                 wr_ptr <= wr_ptr_next;
    77                                                           // Binary to Gray conversion: XOR shifted value with original
    78              1                    4139612                 wr_ptr_gray <= wr_ptr_next ^ (wr_ptr_next >> 1); 
    79                                                       end
    80                                                   end
    81                                               
    82                                                   // =========================================================================
    83                                                   // 2. Memory Write Operation (DFT-Compliant)
    84                                                   // Description: Writes data to the memory array. Incorporates a full reset 
    85                                                   //              loop to synthesize into standard D-Flip-Flops with reset 
    86                                                   //              pins, ensuring compatibility with DFT scan chains.
    87                                                   // =========================================================================
    88              1                    8279551         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    89                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    90              1                        218                 for (j = 0; j < DEPTH; j = j + 1) begin
    90              2                       3488     
    91              1                       3488                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    92                                                           end
    93                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    94              1                    4139612                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    95                                                       end
    96                                                   end
    97                                               
    98                                                   // =========================================================================
    99                                                   // 3. Read Domain Logic
    100                                                  // Description: Manages the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    101                                                  // =========================================================================
    102             1                   12418998         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    103                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    104             1                        108                 rd_ptr <= 0;
    105             1                        108                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    106                                                      end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    107             1                    4139612                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;
    108                                                          // Binary to Gray conversion
    109             1                    4139612                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    110                                                      end
    111                                                  end
    112                                                  
    113                                                  // Continuous assignment for data output, gated by the 'o_empty' flag
    114                                                  // to prevent reading garbage/stale data.
    115             1                   12421303         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    116                                              
    117                                                  // =========================================================================
    118                                                  // 4. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    119                                                  // Description: Synchronizes Gray-coded pointers into the opposite domain
    120                                                  //              using standard 2-FF synchronizers to mitigate metastability.
    121                                                  // =========================================================================
    122                                                  
    123                                                  // Synchronize Read Pointer into Write Clock Domain
    124             1                   12419163         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    125                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    126             1                        218                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    127             1                        218                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    128                                                      end else begin
    129             1                   12418945                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    130             1                   12418945                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    131                                                      end
    132                                                  end
    133                                              
    134                                                  // Synchronize Write Pointer into Read Clock Domain
    135             1                   12418974         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    136                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    137             1                        108                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    138             1                        108                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    139                                                      end else begin
    140             1                   12418866                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    141             1                   12418866                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    142                                                      end
    143                                                  end
    144                                              
    145                                                  // =========================================================================
    146                                                  // 5. Status Flag Generation
    147                                                  // Description: 
    148                                                  //   - o_full : True if write pointer matches synchronized read pointer, 
    149                                                  //              accounting for the wrap-around bit in the Gray domain.
    150                                                  //   - o_empty: True if read pointer matches synchronized write pointer.
    151                                                  // =========================================================================
    152             1                    8279238         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    153             1                    8279238         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);

    85                                                   //              loop to synthesize into standard D-Flip-Flops with reset 
    86                                                   //              pins, ensuring compatibility with DFT scan chains.
    87                                                   // =========================================================================
    88              1                    8279551         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    89                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    90              1                        218                 for (j = 0; j < DEPTH; j = j + 1) begin
    90              2                       3488     
    91              1                       3488                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    92                                                           end
    93                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    94              1                    4139612                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    95                                                       end
    96                                                   end
    97                                               
    98                                                   // =========================================================================
    99                                                   // 3. Read Domain Logic
    100                                                  // Description: Manages the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    101                                                  // =========================================================================
    102             1                   12418998         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    103                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    104             1                        108                 rd_ptr <= 0;
    105             1                        108                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    106                                                      end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    107             1                    4139612                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;
    108                                                          // Binary to Gray conversion
    109             1                    4139612                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    110                                                      end
    111                                                  end
    112                                                  
    113                                                  // Continuous assignment for data output, gated by the 'o_empty' flag
    114                                                  // to prevent reading garbage/stale data.
    115             1                   12421303         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    116                                              
    117                                                  // =========================================================================
    118                                                  // 4. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    119                                                  // Description: Synchronizes Gray-coded pointers into the opposite domain
    120                                                  //              using standard 2-FF synchronizers to mitigate metastability.
    121                                                  // =========================================================================
    122                                                  
    123                                                  // Synchronize Read Pointer into Write Clock Domain
    124             1                   12419163         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    125                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    126             1                        218                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    127             1                        218                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    128                                                      end else begin
    129             1                   12418945                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    130             1                   12418945                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    131                                                      end
    132                                                  end
    133                                              
    134                                                  // Synchronize Write Pointer into Read Clock Domain
    135             1                   12418974         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    136                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    137             1                        108                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    138             1                        108                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    139                                                      end else begin
    140             1                   12418866                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    141             1                   12418866                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    142                                                      end
    143                                                  end
    144                                              
    145                                                  // =========================================================================
    146                                                  // 5. Status Flag Generation
    147                                                  // Description: 
    148                                                  //   - o_full : True if write pointer matches synchronized read pointer, 
    149                                                  //              accounting for the wrap-around bit in the Gray domain.
    150                                                  //   - o_empty: True if read pointer matches synchronized write pointer.
    151                                                  // =========================================================================
    152             1                    8279238         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    153             1                    8279238         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);

    14                                               module ppi_tx (
    15                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------

    36

    39                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    40                                                   output reg         o_TxClkRequestHS,
    41                                                   input  wire        i_TxClkReadyHS,
    42                                                   input  wire        i_TxByteClkHS,     
    43

    48                                                   output wire        o_lane0_valid_hs,

    50                                                   output wire        o_lane1_valid_hs,
    51                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_lane2_data_hs, 
    52                                                   output wire        o_lane2_valid_hs,
    53                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_lane3_data_hs, 
    54                                                   output wire        o_lane3_valid_hs,
    55                                               
    56                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    57                                                   // MIPI D-PHY PPI Control & Low-Power (ULPM) Signals
    58                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    59                                                   input  wire        i_Shutdown,
    60                                                   input  wire        i_TxClkEsc,
    61                                                   output wire        o_TxUlpmClk,
    62                                                   output wire [3:0]  o_TxRequestEsc,
    63                                                   output wire [3:0]  o_TxUlpmEsc
    64                                               );
    65                                               
    66                                                   // =========================================================================
    67                                                   // 1. High-Speed Clock Request & Keep-Alive Logic
    68                                                   // Concept: Ensures the HS clock remains stable and active for at least 
    69                                                   //          TCLK-POST time after the packet request drops, preventing jitter 
    70                                                   //          and ensuring clean packet termination at the D-PHY level.
    71                                                   // Note:    Targeting 320 Mbps (Lane) -> clk_sys = 160 MHz (6.25ns).
    72                                                   //          TCLK-POST required ~222.5ns. 40 cycles * 6.25ns = 250ns (Safe margin).
    73                                                   // =========================================================================
    74                                                   reg [5:0] keep_alive_cnt; 
    75              1                     238520         always @(posedge i_clk_sys or negedge i_rst_n_sys) begin
    76                                                       if (!i_rst_n_sys) begin
    77              1                        218                 keep_alive_cnt   <= 6'd0;
    78              1                        218                 o_TxClkRequestHS <= 1'b0;
    79                                                       end else begin
    80                                                           if (i_req_hs) begin
    81              1                      10999                     o_TxClkRequestHS <= 1'b1;
    82              1                      10999                     keep_alive_cnt   <= 6'd40; // Modified from 31 to 40 for 320 Mbps Specs
    83                                                           end else if (keep_alive_cnt > 0) begin
    84              1                     216390                     o_TxClkRequestHS <= 1'b1;
    85              1                     216390                     keep_alive_cnt   <= keep_alive_cnt - 1;
    86                                                           end else begin
    87              1                      10913                     o_TxClkRequestHS <= 1'b0;
    88                                                           end
    89                                                       end
    90                                                   end
    91                                               
    92                                                   // =========================================================================
    93                                                   // 2. Escape Clock Domain Sink (Synthesis Workaround)
    94                                                   // Concept: Implements a dummy flip-flop to act as a sequential sink for 
    95                                                   //          'i_TxClkEsc', satisfying EDA tools (e.g., SpyGlass PCLK04) 
    96                                                   //          while maintaining a strict ASIC-compliant synthesis path.
    97                                                   // =========================================================================
    98                                                   reg r_dummy_esc_ff; 
    99              1                    ***0***         always @(posedge i_TxClkEsc) begin
    100             1                    ***0***             r_dummy_esc_ff <= ~r_dummy_esc_ff; 
    101                                                  end
    102                                              
    103                                                  // =========================================================================
    104                                                  // 3. CIL Power Management Logic (ULPM Control)
    105                                                  // Concept: Dynamically triggers Ultra Low Power Mode (ULPM) when the 
    106                                                  //          controller is idle and not requesting High-Speed mode.
    107                                                  // =========================================================================
    108             1                      21831         wire w_is_idle = ~(o_TxClkRequestHS | i_req_hs);
    109             1                      10807         assign o_TxUlpmClk    = w_is_idle & ~i_Shutdown;
    110             1                      10807         assign o_TxRequestEsc = {4{w_is_idle & ~i_Shutdown}};
    111             1                      10807         assign o_TxUlpmEsc    = {4{w_is_idle & ~i_Shutdown}};
    112                                              
    113                                                  // =========================================================================
    114                                                  // 4. Data Path & Clock Domain Crossing (CDC)
    115                                                  // Architecture: 4 Independent Asynchronous FIFOs to safely transport data 
    116                                                  //               from the System Clock domain to the D-PHY HS Clock domain.
    117                                                  // =========================================================================
    118                                                  wire w_empty_0, w_empty_1, w_empty_2, w_empty_3;
    119                                                  wire [7:0] w_raw_data_0, w_raw_data_1, w_raw_data_2, w_raw_data_3;
    120                                                  wire w_fifo_full_0, w_fifo_full_1, w_fifo_full_2, w_fifo_full_3;
    121                                              
    122                                                  // Read enables gated by PPI Ready signals
    123             1                   22134651         wire w_rd_en_0 = o_TxRequestHS[0] & i_TxReadyHS[0];

    158                                                  );
    159                                              
    160                                                  // =========================================================================
    161                                                  // 5. Synthesis Optimization & Structural Sink
    162                                                  // Concept: Uses an AND-reduction with 1'b0 to create a safe structural 
    163                                                  //          sink for unused internal status signals, ensuring the RTL 
    164                                                  //          remains clean of floating nets during gate-level synthesis.
    165                                                  // =========================================================================
    166             1                         48         wire w_dummy_sink = &{1'b0, i_active_lanes, w_fifo_full_0, w_fifo_full_1, w_fifo_full_2, w_fifo_full_3, r_dummy_esc_ff, i_Shutdown};
    167                                              
    168                                                  // The dummy sink is logically OR'ed with o_TxRequestHS[0] (X | 0 = X)
    169             1                   11078132         assign o_TxRequestHS[0] = (i_TxClkReadyHS & ~w_empty_0) | w_dummy_sink;
    170             1                   11075121         assign o_TxRequestHS[1] = i_TxClkReadyHS & ~w_empty_1;
    171             1                    8290031         assign o_TxRequestHS[2] = i_TxClkReadyHS & ~w_empty_2;
    172             1                    8290031         assign o_TxRequestHS[3] = i_TxClkReadyHS & ~w_empty_3;
    173                                              
    174                                                  // Output assignments & valid gating
    175                                                  assign o_lane0_valid_hs = w_rd_en_0;
    176                                                  assign o_lane1_valid_hs = w_rd_en_1;
    177                                                  assign o_lane2_valid_hs = w_rd_en_2;
    178                                                  assign o_lane3_valid_hs = w_rd_en_3;
