    29                                                   output wire        ppi_tx_ValidHS_0, ppi_tx_ValidHS_1, ppi_tx_ValidHS_2, ppi_tx_ValidHS_3,
    30                                               
    31                                                   // Debug Taps
    32                                                   output wire [7:0]  tx_lane0_data_out, tx_lane1_data_out, tx_lane2_data_out, tx_lane3_data_out,
    33                                                   output wire        tx_lane0_vld_out, tx_lane1_vld_out, tx_lane2_vld_out, tx_lane3_vld_out,
    34                                                   output wire        tx_req_hs_out
    35                                               );
    36                                               
    37                                                   // =========================================================================
    38                                                   // 0. INTERNAL WIRES DECLARATION
    39                                                   // =========================================================================
    40                                                   wire w_tx_en, w_sw_rst, w_scram_enb, w_crc_enb;
    41                                                   wire [15:0] w_cfg_wc, w_i2c_line_number;
    42                                                   wire [1:0]  w_cfg_vc_id, w_cfg_active_lanes_i2c;
    43                                                   wire [5:0]  w_cfg_data_type;
    44                                                   wire w_pkt_valid, w_byte_pd;
    45                                               
    46                                                   // =========================================================================
    47                                                   // 1. Hardware Reset Synchronizers (Async Assert, Sync De-assert)
    48                                                   // Description: Prevents recovery metastability by ensuring resets are 
    49                                                   //              de-asserted synchronously to their respective clock domains.
    50                                                   // Note: Separate reset ports are strictly maintained for ModelSim TB compatibility.
    51                                                   // =========================================================================
    52                                                   wire w_presetn_sync;
    53                                                   reg [1:0] sync_regs_apb;
    54              1                        275         always @(posedge pclk or negedge preset_n) begin
    55              1                        110             if (!preset_n) sync_regs_apb <= 2'b00; else sync_regs_apb <= {sync_regs_apb[0], 1'b1};
    55              2                        165     
    56                                                   end
    57                                                   assign w_presetn_sync = sync_regs_apb[1];
    58                                               
    59                                                   wire w_rst_n_pix;
    60                                                   reg [1:0] sync_regs_pix;
    61              1                        275         always @(posedge pixel_clk or negedge pixel_rst_n) begin
    62              1                        110             if (!pixel_rst_n) sync_regs_pix <= 2'b00; else sync_regs_pix <= {sync_regs_pix[0], 1'b1};
    62              2                        165     
    63                                                   end
    64                                                   assign w_rst_n_pix = sync_regs_pix[1];
    65                                               
    66                                                   wire w_rst_n_sys;
    67                                                   reg [1:0] sync_regs_sys;
    68              1                        275         always @(posedge sys_clk or negedge sys_rst_n) begin
    69              1                        110             if (!sys_rst_n) sync_regs_sys <= 2'b00; else sync_regs_sys <= {sync_regs_sys[0], 1'b1};
    69              2                        165     
    70                                                   end
    71                                                   assign w_rst_n_sys = sync_regs_sys[1];
    72                                               
    73                                                   wire w_rst_n_byte;
    74                                                   reg [1:0] sync_regs_byte;
    75              1                        275         always @(posedge byte_clk or negedge byte_rst_n) begin
    76              1                        110             if (!byte_rst_n) sync_regs_byte <= 2'b00; else sync_regs_byte <= {sync_regs_byte[0], 1'b1};
    76              2                        165     
    77                                                   end
    78                                                   assign w_rst_n_byte = sync_regs_byte[1];
    79                                               
    80                                                   wire w_rst_n_tx;
    81                                                   reg [1:0] sync_regs_tx;
    82              1                        217         always @(posedge ppi_tx_ByteClkHS or negedge sys_rst_n) begin
    83              1                         55             if (!sys_rst_n) sync_regs_tx <= 2'b00; else sync_regs_tx <= {sync_regs_tx[0], 1'b1};
    83              2                        162     
    84                                                   end
    85                                                   assign w_rst_n_tx = sync_regs_tx[1];
    86                                               
    87                                                   // =========================================================================
    88                                                   // 2. Software Reset Injection (Safe De-assertion)
    89                                                   // =========================================================================
    90                                                   reg [1:0] sync_sw_rst_pix, sync_sw_rst_sys, sync_sw_rst_byte, sync_sw_rst_tx;
    91                                                   
    92              1                        264         always @(posedge pixel_clk or negedge w_rst_n_pix) begin
    93              1                        110             if (!w_rst_n_pix) sync_sw_rst_pix <= 2'b00; else sync_sw_rst_pix <= {sync_sw_rst_pix[0], w_sw_rst};
    93              2                        154     
    94                                                   end
    95              1                        444         always @(posedge sys_clk or negedge w_rst_n_sys) begin
    96              1                        110             if (!w_rst_n_sys) sync_sw_rst_sys <= 2'b00; else sync_sw_rst_sys <= {sync_sw_rst_sys[0], w_sw_rst};
    96              2                        334     
    97                                                   end
    98              1                        273         always @(posedge byte_clk or negedge w_rst_n_byte) begin
    99              1                        110             if (!w_rst_n_byte) sync_sw_rst_byte <= 2'b00; else sync_sw_rst_byte <= {sync_sw_rst_byte[0], w_sw_rst};
    99              2                        163     
    100                                                  end
    101             1                        162         always @(posedge ppi_tx_ByteClkHS or negedge w_rst_n_tx) begin
    102             1                        108             if (!w_rst_n_tx) sync_sw_rst_tx <= 2'b00; else sync_sw_rst_tx <= {sync_sw_rst_tx[0], w_sw_rst};
    102             2                         54     
    103                                                  end
    104                                              
    105             1                        157         wire final_rst_n_pix  = w_rst_n_pix  & ~sync_sw_rst_pix[1];
    106             1                        313         wire final_rst_n_sys  = w_rst_n_sys  & ~sync_sw_rst_sys[1];
    107             1                        166         wire final_rst_n_byte = w_rst_n_byte & ~sync_sw_rst_byte[1];
    108             1                        110         wire final_rst_n_tx   = w_rst_n_tx   & ~sync_sw_rst_tx[1];
    109                                              
    110                                                  // =========================================================================
    111                                                  // 3. Control Signal Synchronizers
    112                                                  // =========================================================================
    113                                                  reg [1:0] pix_sync_tx_en;
    114                                                  reg [5:0] pix_cfg_dt_sync1, pix_cfg_dt_sync2;
    115                                                  
    116             1                        495         always @(posedge pixel_clk or negedge final_rst_n_pix) begin
    117                                                      if (!final_rst_n_pix) begin
    118             1                        140                 pix_sync_tx_en   <= 2'b00;
    119             1                        140                 pix_cfg_dt_sync1 <= 6'h2A;
    120             1                        140                 pix_cfg_dt_sync2 <= 6'h2A;
    121                                                      end else begin
    122             1                        355                 pix_sync_tx_en   <= {pix_sync_tx_en[0], w_tx_en};
    123             1                        355                 pix_cfg_dt_sync1 <= w_cfg_data_type;
    124             1                        355                 pix_cfg_dt_sync2 <= pix_cfg_dt_sync1;
    125                                                      end
    126                                                  end
    127                                              
    128                                                  reg [1:0] byte_sync_crc_enb;
    129             1                        306         always @(posedge byte_clk or negedge final_rst_n_byte) begin
    130             1                        146             if (!final_rst_n_byte) byte_sync_crc_enb <= 2'b00;
    131             1                        160             else                   byte_sync_crc_enb <= {byte_sync_crc_enb[0], w_crc_enb};
    132                                                  end
    133                                              
    134                                                  // =========================================================================
    135                                                  // 4. Dummy Wires Declaration
    136                                                  // =========================================================================
    137                                                  wire w_dummy_pslverr, w_dummy_tx_active, w_dummy_fifo_thresh, w_dummy_fifo_stat;
    138                                                  wire o_frame_end, w_dummy_ulpm_clk;
    139                                                  wire [3:0] w_dummy_req_esc, w_dummy_ulpm_esc;
    140                                              
    141                                                  // =========================================================================
    142                                                  // 5. Modules Instantiation
    143                                                  // =========================================================================
    144                                                  apb_csi2_tx_regfile u_regfile (
    145                                                      .PCLK(pclk), .PRESETn(w_presetn_sync), .PSEL(tx_apb_psel), .PENABLE(tx_apb_penable), .PWRITE(tx_apb_pwrite), .PSTRB(4'hF),
    146                                                      .PADDR(tx_apb_paddr), .PWDATA(tx_apb_pwdata), .PRDATA(tx_apb_prdata), .PREADY(tx_apb_pready),
    147                                                      .PSLVERR(w_dummy_pslverr), .tx_en(w_tx_en), .sw_rst(w_sw_rst), .scram_enb(w_scram_enb), .crc_enb(w_crc_enb)
    148                                                  );
    149                                              
    150                                                  I2C_top_cci_csi u_i2c_cci (
    151                                                      .i_clk(byte_clk), .i_rst_n(final_rst_n_byte), .i_scl(i2c_scl), .io_sda(i2c_sda),
    152                                                      .i_packet_valid(w_pkt_valid), .i_end_of_packet(w_byte_pd),
    153                                                      .o_word_count(w_cfg_wc), .o_virtual_channel(w_cfg_vc_id), .o_data_type(w_cfg_data_type),
    154                                                      .o_lane_count(w_cfg_active_lanes_i2c), .o_line_number(w_i2c_line_number), .o_tx_active(w_dummy_tx_active)
    155                                                  );
    156                                              
    157                                                  wire [23:0] w_native_data;
    158                                                  wire w_native_valid, w_native_ready, w_native_sof, w_native_eol;
    159                                              
    160                                                  axi_streaming_tx u_axi_tx (
    161                                                      .i_pixel_clk(pixel_clk), .i_pixel_rst_n(final_rst_n_pix), 
    162                                                      .i_byte_clk(byte_clk),   .i_byte_rst_n(final_rst_n_byte),
    163                                                      .i_cfg_data_type(pix_cfg_dt_sync2), // Synced
    164                                                      .i_axis_tdata(tx_axis_tdata), 
    165                                                      .i_axis_tvalid(tx_axis_tvalid & pix_sync_tx_en[1]), // Synced
    166                                                      .o_axis_tready(tx_axis_tready), .i_axis_tuser(tx_axis_tuser), .i_axis_tlast(tx_axis_tlast),
    167                                                      .o_native_data(w_native_data), .o_native_valid(w_native_valid), .i_native_ready(w_native_ready),
    168                                                      .o_native_sof(w_native_sof), .o_native_eol(w_native_eol), .o_fifo_threshold_met(w_dummy_fifo_thresh), .o_fifo_stat(w_dummy_fifo_stat)
    169                                                  );
    170                                              
    171                                                  wire [7:0] w_byte_data; wire w_byte_valid, w_byte_fs, w_byte_fe, w_byte_ls;
    172                                                  pixel2byte_converter u_p2b (
    173                                                      .i_byte_clk(byte_clk), .i_rst_n(final_rst_n_byte), .i_cfg_data_type(w_cfg_data_type),
    174                                                      .i_frame_num_lines(w_i2c_line_number), .i_native_data(w_native_data), .i_native_valid(w_native_valid),
    175                                                      .o_native_ready(w_native_ready), .i_native_sof(w_native_sof), .i_native_eol(w_native_eol),
    176                                                      .o_byte_data(w_byte_data), .o_byte_valid(w_byte_valid), .o_byte_frame_start(w_byte_fs),
    177                                                      .o_byte_frame_end(w_byte_fe), .o_byte_line_start(w_byte_ls), .o_byte_packet_done(w_byte_pd)
    178                                                  );
    179                                              
    180                                                  // =========================================================================
    181                                                  // ECC TX: separate computation for long packets and short packets.
    182                                                  //
    183                                                  // BUG (before fix): a single csi2_ecc_tx was permanently wired to the
    184                                                  // long-packet configuration (cfg_data_type, cfg_wc). When the packetizer
    185                                                  // emits a short packet (Frame Start / Frame End), it sends:
    186                                                  //   byte0 = {VC, 6'h00 or 6'h01}   (DI for FS/FE)
    187                                                  //   byte1 = 0x00
    188                                                  //   byte2 = 0x00
    189                                                  //   byte3 = {2'b00, ecc}
    190                                                  // The WC field bytes are 0x00 in the actual short-packet header, but
    191                                                  // the old ECC was computed from cfg_wc=1000.  At the receiver:
    192                                                  //   d_rx   = {2'b00, 0x00, 0x00, DI_short}  (all data bytes = 0 for WC)
    193                                                  //   ecc_rx = ECC computed from cfg_wc=1000
    194                                                  //   syndrome = ecc_rx ^ ecc_calc(d_rx) != 0  --> false error flag.
    195                                                  //
    196                                                  // For RAW8 (DI=0x2A, WC=1000): syndrome=0x19 -> false SINGLE BIT ERROR.
    197                                                  // This is exactly the "[ECC Single-Bit Error CORRECTED]" seen in the log
    198                                                  // on every Frame Start / Frame End packet.
    199                                                  //
    200                                                  // FIX: use two ECC instances muxed by w_byte_fs / o_frame_end:
    201                                                  //   Long  packet ECC: uses {VC, cfg_data_type, cfg_wc}   (unchanged)
    202                                                  //   Short packet ECC: uses {VC, DT_short=0,   WC=0}
    203                                                  // The packetizer selects i_ecc correctly because we pass the muxed output.
    204                                                  // =========================================================================
    205                                              
    206                                                  // Long-packet ECC (unchanged behaviour for S_HEADER state)
    207                                                  wire [5:0] w_ecc_long;
    208             1                        245         wire [25:0] w_header_data_long = { 2'b00, w_cfg_wc[15:8], w_cfg_wc[7:0], w_cfg_vc_id, w_cfg_data_type };
    209                                                  csi2_ecc_tx u_ecc_long (.i_header_data(w_header_data_long), .o_ecc(w_ecc_long));
    210                                              
    211                                                  // Short-packet ECC: DI encodes only VC (DT bits = 0x00 for FS, 0x01 for FE),
    212                                                  // WC field = 0.  Use 0x00 DT for Frame Start; the packetizer latches the
    213                                                  // correct DI byte internally (r_short_di), so the ECC only needs to match
    214                                                  // the bytes it actually sends: {r_short_di, 0x00, 0x00, ECC}.
    215                                                  // Both FS and FE have WC=0 and DT[5:4]=2'b00, so the same ECC instance
    216                                                  // covers both (they only differ in DT[1:0] which we encode via w_byte_fs).
    217                                                  wire [5:0] w_ecc_fs, w_ecc_fe;
    218                                                 // Line 218 — Frame Start short packet:
    219             1                          2     wire [25:0] w_header_data_fs = { 2'b00, 8'h00, 8'h00, w_cfg_vc_id, 6'h00 };
    220                                              
    221                                              // Line 219 — Frame End short packet:
    222             1                          2     wire [25:0] w_header_data_fe = { 2'b00, 8'h00, 8'h00, w_cfg_vc_id, 6'h01 };
    223                                                 
    224                                                  csi2_ecc_tx u_ecc_fs (.i_header_data(w_header_data_fs), .o_ecc(w_ecc_fs));
    225                                                  csi2_ecc_tx u_ecc_fe (.i_header_data(w_header_data_fe), .o_ecc(w_ecc_fe));
    226                                              
    227                                                  // Mux: select the correct ECC based on which packet type is being sent.
    228                                                  // w_byte_fs  = frame-start signal entering the packetizer
    229                                                  // o_frame_end = frame-end signal (delayed by fe_reg chain in packetizer)
    230                                                  // When neither is active, use the long-packet ECC.
    231             1                        467         wire [5:0] w_ecc_out = w_byte_fs   ? w_ecc_fs   :
    232                                                                          o_frame_end ? w_ecc_fe   :
    233                                                                                       w_ecc_long;
    234                                              
    235                                                  wire [7:0] w_crc_out; wire w_crc_valid;
    236                                                  crc_tx u_crc (
    237                                                      .clk(byte_clk), .reset_n(final_rst_n_byte), .crc_en(byte_sync_crc_enb[1]), .i_data_valid(w_byte_valid), // Synced
    238                                                      .i_data_in(w_byte_data), .i_start(w_byte_ls), .i_end(w_byte_pd), .o_valid(w_crc_valid), .o_data(w_crc_out)
    239                                                  );
    240                                              
    241                                                  wire [7:0] w_pkt_data;
    242                                                  csi2_packetizer u_packetizer (
    243                                                      .i_byte_clk(byte_clk), .i_rst_n(final_rst_n_byte), .i_p2b_data(w_byte_data), .i_p2b_valid(w_byte_valid),
    244                                                      .i_p2b_line_start(w_byte_ls), .i_p2b_packet_done(w_byte_pd), .i_p2b_frame_start(w_byte_fs),
    245                                                      .i_p2b_frame_end(w_byte_fe), .i_cfg_wc(w_cfg_wc), .i_cfg_data_type(w_cfg_data_type),
    246                                                      .i_cfg_vc_id(w_cfg_vc_id), .i_ecc(w_ecc_out), .i_crc_data(w_crc_out), .i_cfg_crc_en(byte_sync_crc_enb[1]), // Synced
    247                                                      .o_tx_data(w_pkt_data), .o_tx_valid(w_pkt_valid), .o_frame_end(o_frame_end)
    248                                                  );
    249                                              
    250             1                         47         wire [1:0] w_dist_cfg_lanes = (cfg_active_lanes == 3'd4) ? 2'b11 : (cfg_active_lanes == 3'd2) ? 2'b01 : 2'b00;
    251                                                  wire [7:0] w_dist_data_0, w_dist_data_1, w_dist_data_2, w_dist_data_3;
    252                                                  wire w_dist_vld_0, w_dist_vld_1, w_dist_vld_2, w_dist_vld_3;
    253                                                  
    254                                                  lane_distributor u_dist (
    255                                                      .i_clk(sys_clk), .i_rst_n(final_rst_n_sys),
    256                                                      .i_pkt_data(w_pkt_data), .i_pkt_vld(w_pkt_valid),
    257                                                      .i_cfg_active_lanes(w_dist_cfg_lanes),
    258                                                      .o_lane0_data(w_dist_data_0), .o_lane1_data(w_dist_data_1), .o_lane2_data(w_dist_data_2), .o_lane3_data(w_dist_data_3),
    259                                                      .o_lane0_vld(w_dist_vld_0),   .o_lane1_vld(w_dist_vld_1),   .o_lane2_vld(w_dist_vld_2),   .o_lane3_vld(w_dist_vld_3),
    260                                                      .o_req_hs(tx_req_hs_out)
    261                                                  );
    262                                              
    263                                                  reg r_tx_active_ext; reg [3:0] tx_hang_timer;
    264             1                     103605         always @(posedge sys_clk or negedge final_rst_n_sys) begin 
    265             1                        218             if (!final_rst_n_sys) begin r_tx_active_ext <= 0; tx_hang_timer <= 0; end 
    265             2                        218     
    266                                                      else begin
    267             1                      11024                 if (w_pkt_valid) begin r_tx_active_ext <= 1; tx_hang_timer <= 4'd15; end 
    267             2                      11024     
    268             1                      81450                 else if (tx_hang_timer > 0) begin tx_hang_timer <= tx_hang_timer - 1; end 
    269             1                      10913                 else begin r_tx_active_ext <= 0; end
    270                                                      end
    271                                                  end
    272                                              
    273                                                  // Scramblers match ModelSim functionally (w_scram_enb kept un-synced here to preserve exact testbench behavior, only fed to lint sink as before)
    274                                                  mipi_scrambler #(.LANE_SEED(16'h0810)) u_scr_tx0 (.i_clk(sys_clk), .i_rst_n(final_rst_n_sys), .i_packet_active(r_tx_active_ext), .i_lane_data(w_dist_data_0), .i_lane_vld(w_dist_vld_0), .o_scrambled_data(tx_lane0_data_out), .o_lane_vld_out(tx_lane0_vld_out));
    275                                                  mipi_scrambler #(.LANE_SEED(16'h0990)) u_scr_tx1 (.i_clk(sys_clk), .i_rst_n(final_rst_n_sys), .i_packet_active(r_tx_active_ext), .i_lane_data(w_dist_data_1), .i_lane_vld(w_dist_vld_1), .o_scrambled_data(tx_lane1_data_out), .o_lane_vld_out(tx_lane1_vld_out));
    276                                                  mipi_scrambler #(.LANE_SEED(16'h0A51)) u_scr_tx2 (.i_clk(sys_clk), .i_rst_n(final_rst_n_sys), .i_packet_active(r_tx_active_ext), .i_lane_data(w_dist_data_2), .i_lane_vld(w_dist_vld_2), .o_scrambled_data(tx_lane2_data_out), .o_lane_vld_out(tx_lane2_vld_out));
    277                                                  mipi_scrambler #(.LANE_SEED(16'h0BD0)) u_scr_tx3 (.i_clk(sys_clk), .i_rst_n(final_rst_n_sys), .i_packet_active(r_tx_active_ext), .i_lane_data(w_dist_data_3), .i_lane_vld(w_dist_vld_3), .o_scrambled_data(tx_lane3_data_out), .o_lane_vld_out(tx_lane3_vld_out));
    278                                              
    279                                                  ppi_tx u_tx (
    280                                                      .i_clk_sys(sys_clk), .i_rst_n_sys(final_rst_n_sys), .i_rst_n_tx(final_rst_n_tx), 
    281                                                      .i_active_lanes(cfg_active_lanes),
    282                                                      .i_lane0_data(tx_lane0_data_out), .i_lane0_vld(tx_lane0_vld_out),
    283                                                      .i_lane1_data(tx_lane1_data_out), .i_lane1_vld(tx_lane1_vld_out),
    284                                                      .i_lane2_data(tx_lane2_data_out), .i_lane2_vld(tx_lane2_vld_out),
    285                                                      .i_lane3_data(tx_lane3_data_out), .i_lane3_vld(tx_lane3_vld_out),
    286                                                      .i_req_hs(tx_req_hs_out),
    287                                                      
    288                                                      .i_Shutdown(1'b0), .i_TxClkEsc(1'b0), .o_TxUlpmClk(w_dummy_ulpm_clk), .o_TxRequestEsc(w_dummy_req_esc), .o_TxUlpmEsc(w_dummy_ulpm_esc),
    289                                                      .o_TxClkRequestHS(ppi_tx_ClkRequestHS), .i_TxClkReadyHS(ppi_tx_ClkReadyHS), .i_TxByteClkHS(ppi_tx_ByteClkHS),
    290                                                      .o_TxRequestHS(ppi_tx_RequestHS), .i_TxReadyHS(ppi_tx_ReadyHS),
    291                                                      .o_lane0_data_hs(ppi_tx_DataHS_0), .o_lane0_valid_hs(ppi_tx_ValidHS_0),
    292                                                      .o_lane1_data_hs(ppi_tx_DataHS_1), .o_lane1_valid_hs(ppi_tx_ValidHS_1),
    293                                                      .o_lane2_data_hs(ppi_tx_DataHS_2), .o_lane2_valid_hs(ppi_tx_ValidHS_2),
    294                                                      .o_lane3_data_hs(ppi_tx_DataHS_3), .o_lane3_valid_hs(ppi_tx_ValidHS_3)
    295                                                  );
    296                                              
    297                                                  // =========================================================================

    Line         Item                      Count     Source

  File rx_regfile.v
    30                                               module apb_csi2_rx_regfile #(
    31                                                   parameter integer APB_ADDR_WIDTH = 32
    32                                               ) (
    33                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34                                                   // APB Clock and Reset
    35                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    36                                                   input  wire                      PCLK,           // APB clock (rising-edge)
    37                                                   input  wire                      PRESETn,        // APB reset, active-LOW
    38                                               
    39                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    40                                                   // APB Slave Interface
    41                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    42                                                   input  wire [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] PADDR,          // Byte address
    43                                                   input  wire                      PSEL,           // Peripheral select
    44                                                   input  wire                      PENABLE,        // ACCESS phase strobe
    45                                                   input  wire                      PWRITE,         // 1=Write  0=Read
    46                                                   input  wire [31:0]               PWDATA,         // Write data
    47                                                   input  wire [3:0]                PSTRB,          // Write byte strobes
    48                                               
    49                                                   output reg  [31:0]               PRDATA,         // Read data
    50                                                   output wire                      PREADY,         // Slave ready 
    51                                                   output wire                      PSLVERR,        // Slave error
    52                                               
    53                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    54                                                   // Register outputs to CSI-2 RX 
    55                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    56                                                   output reg                       rx_en,          // RX_CTRL[0] - RX Enable
    57                                                   output reg                       sw_rst,         // RX_CTRL[1] - SW Reset pulse
    58                                                   output reg                       rx_scram_enb,   // RX_CFG[0]  - Scramble Enable
    59                                                   output reg                       rx_crc_enb,     // RX_CFG[1]  - CRC Enable
    60                                               
    61                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62                                                   // error inputs from CSI-2 RX 
    63                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64                                                   input  wire                      hw_crc_err,     // RX_ERR[0] - CRC error 
    65                                                   input  wire                      hw_ecc_err,     // RX_ERR[1] - ECC error 
    66                                                   input  wire                      hw_cecc_err     // RX_ERR[2] - Corrected ECC 
    67                                               );
    68                                               
    69                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    70                                                   // Local Parameters - Register Addresses 
    71                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72                                                   localparam [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] ADDR_RX_CTRL = 'h000;
    73                                                   localparam [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] ADDR_RX_CFG  = 'h004;
    74                                                   localparam [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] ADDR_RX_ERR  = 'h00C;
    75                                               
    76                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77                                                   // Internal Wires
    78                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    79                                                   wire wr_en;
    80                                                   wire rd_en;
    81                                                   wire sel_rx_ctrl;
    82                                                   wire sel_rx_cfg;
    83                                                   wire sel_rx_err;
    84                                                   wire addr_valid;
    85                                               
    86              1                        488         assign wr_en = PSEL & PENABLE &  PWRITE;
    87              1                        488         assign rd_en = PSEL & PENABLE & ~PWRITE;
    88                                               
    89              1                        110         assign sel_rx_ctrl = (PADDR[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2] == ADDR_RX_CTRL[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2]);
    90              1                        110         assign sel_rx_cfg  = (PADDR[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2] == ADDR_RX_CFG [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2]);
    91              1                        110         assign sel_rx_err  = (PADDR[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2] == ADDR_RX_ERR [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2]);
    92              1                        110         assign addr_valid  = sel_rx_ctrl | sel_rx_cfg | sel_rx_err;
    93                                               
    94                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    95                                                   // APB Response Signals
    96                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    97                                                   assign PREADY  = 1'b1;
    98              1                        489         assign PSLVERR = PSEL & PENABLE & ~addr_valid;
    99                                               
    100                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    101                                                  // Sticky error registers
    102                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    103                                                  reg crc_err_sticky;
    104                                                  reg ecc_err_sticky;
    105                                                  reg cecc_err_sticky;
    106                                              
    107                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    108                                                  // R/W Register Write Logic
    109                                                  //
    110                                                  // Priority (highest to lowest):
    111                                                  //   1. PRESETn LOW       - async reset clears all R/W registers
    112                                                  //   2. SW_RST self-clear - takes priority over APB write in same cycle
    113                                                  //   3. APB write         - only fires when SW_RST is not self-clearing
    114                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    115             1                        705         always @(posedge PCLK or negedge PRESETn) begin
    116                                                      if (!PRESETn) begin
    117             1                        110                 rx_en        <= 1'b0;
    118             1                        110                 sw_rst       <= 1'b0;
    119             1                        110                 rx_scram_enb <= 1'b0;
    120             1                        110                 rx_crc_enb   <= 1'b0;
    121                                              
    122                                                      end else if (sw_rst) begin
    123                                                          //------------------------------------------------------------------
    124                                                          // SW_RST self-clear: highest priority after reset.
    125                                                          //------------------------------------------------------------------
    126             1                         54                 sw_rst <= 1'b0;
    127                                              
    128                                                      end else if (wr_en) begin
    129                                                          //------------------------------------------------------------------
    130                                                          // APB Write: only reached when sw_rst is not self-clearing.
    131                                                          //------------------------------------------------------------------
    132                                              
    133                                                          // RX_CTRL @ 0x000
    134                                                          if (sel_rx_ctrl && PSTRB[0]) begin
    135             1                        108                     rx_en  <= PWDATA[0];    // [0] RX_EN
    136             1                        108                     sw_rst <= PWDATA[1];    // [1] SW_RST
    137                                                              // [31:2] reserved
    138                                                          end
    139                                              
    140                                                          // RX_CFG @ 0x004
    141                                                          if (sel_rx_cfg && PSTRB[0]) begin
    142             1                         54                     rx_scram_enb <= PWDATA[0]; // [0] RX_SCRAM_ENB
    143             1                         54                     rx_crc_enb   <= PWDATA[1]; // [1] RX_CRC_ENB
    144                                                              // [31:2] reserved
    145                                                          end
    146                                              
    147                                                      end
    148                                                  end
    149                                              
    150                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    151                                                  // RX_ERR Sticky Bit Logic
    152                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    153             1                        175         always @(posedge PCLK or negedge PRESETn) begin
    154                                                      if (!PRESETn) begin
    155             1                        110                 crc_err_sticky  <= 1'b0;
    156             1                        110                 ecc_err_sticky  <= 1'b0;
    157             1                        110                 cecc_err_sticky <= 1'b0;
    158                                                      end else begin
    159             1                          3                 if (hw_crc_err)  crc_err_sticky  <= 1'b1;
    160             1                          2                 if (hw_ecc_err)  ecc_err_sticky  <= 1'b1;
    161             1                          2                 if (hw_cecc_err) cecc_err_sticky <= 1'b1;
    162                                                      end
    163                                                  end
    164                                              
    165                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    166                                                  // Register Read Logic
    167                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    168             1                        372         always @(*) begin
    169             1                        372             PRDATA = 32'h0000_0000;
    170                                              
    171                                                      if (rd_en) begin
    172                                                          case (1'b1)
    173             1                    ***0***                     sel_rx_ctrl : PRDATA = {{30{1'b0}}, sw_rst,          rx_en        };
    174             1                    ***0***                     sel_rx_cfg  : PRDATA = {{30{1'b0}}, rx_crc_enb,      rx_scram_enb };
    175             1                    ***0***                     sel_rx_err  : PRDATA = {{29{1'b0}}, cecc_err_sticky,
    176                                                                                                  ecc_err_sticky,
    177                                                                                                  crc_err_sticky              };
    178             1                    ***0***                     default     : PRDATA = 32'h0000_0000;

Statement Coverage for instance /tb_mipi_system_loopback/dut/u_csi2_rx_top/u_rx/u_fifo_l0 --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source

  File async_fifo_ppi_rx.v
    15                                               module async_fifo_ppi_rx #(
    16                                                   parameter WIDTH = 8,
    17                                                   parameter DEPTH = 16
    18                                               )(
    19                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    20                                                   // Write Domain Interface (Source: D-PHY Clock Domain)
    21                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    22                                                   input  wire              i_wr_clk,     // D-PHY Byte Clock
    23                                                   input  wire              i_wr_rst_n,   // Reset synchronized to wr_clk
    24                                                   input  wire              i_wr_en,      // Write enable
    25                                                   input  wire [WIDTH-1:0]  i_din,        // Data input from PPI
    26                                                   output wire              o_full,       // FIFO full flag
    27                                               
    28                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29                                                   // Read Domain Interface (Destination: System Clock Domain)
    30                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   input  wire              i_rd_clk,     // Internal System Clock
    32                                                   input  wire              i_rd_rst_n,   // Reset synchronized to rd_clk
    33                                                   input  wire              i_rd_en,      // Read enable from Lane Merger
    34                                                   output wire [WIDTH-1:0]  o_dout,       // Data output to Lane Merger
    35                                                   output wire              o_empty       // FIFO empty flag
    36                                               );
    37                                               
    38                                                   // Address width calculation
    39                                                   localparam ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH);
    40                                               
    41                                                   // Internal Memory and Pointer Registers
    42                                                   reg [WIDTH-1:0] mem [DEPTH-1:0];
    43                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr, rd_ptr;
    44                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray, rd_ptr_gray; 
    45                                                   
    46                                                   // Cross-Domain Synchronizers (2-Stage / Double-Flop)
    47                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray_sync1, wr_ptr_gray_sync2;
    48                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_gray_sync1, rd_ptr_gray_sync2;
    49                                               
    50                                                   // Pointer increments
    51              1                    9704881         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_next = wr_ptr + 1;
    52              1                    9704881         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_next = rd_ptr + 1;
    53                                                   integer j;
    54                                                   // =========================================================================
    55                                                   // 1. Write Domain Logic
    56                                                   // Description: Updates the write pointer and its Gray-coded version.
    57                                                   //              Writes are only performed when 'i_wr_en' is active and FIFO 
    58                                                   //              is not full. Binary pointer is used for memory addressing.
    59                                                   // =========================================================================
    60              1                   15238676         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    61                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    62              1                        108                 wr_ptr <= 0;
    63              1                        108                 wr_ptr_gray <= 0;
    64                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    65              1                    9704852                 wr_ptr <= wr_ptr_next;
    66              1                    9704852                 wr_ptr_gray <= wr_ptr_next ^ (wr_ptr_next >> 1); 
    67                                                       end
    68                                                   end
    69                                               
    70                                                   // Synthesis of the Memory Array (Register-based)
    71              1                   15238676         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    72                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    73              1                        108                 for (j = 0; j < DEPTH; j = j + 1) begin
    73              2                       1728     
    74              1                       1728                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    75                                                           end
    76                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    77              1                    9704852                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    78                                                       end
    79                                                   end
    80                                               
    81                                                   // =========================================================================
    82                                                   // 2. Read Domain Logic
    83                                                   // Description: Updates the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    84                                                   //              Ensures safe data output only when FIFO is not empty.
    85                                                   //              Gray pointers are used to safely pass the pointer across domains.
    86                                                   // =========================================================================
    87              1                   16677093         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    88                                                       if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    89              1                        110                 rd_ptr <= 0;
    90              1                        110                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    91                                                       end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    92              1                    9704852                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;
    93              1                    9704852                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    94                                                       end
    95                                                   end
    96                                                   
    97                                                   // Data Output Assignment (zeroed if empty to prevent data leakage)
    98              1                   11208104         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    99                                               
    100                                                  // =========================================================================
    101                                                  // 3. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    102                                                  // Description: Synchronizes pointers between the two asynchronous domains.
    103                                                  //              Gray coding ensures only one bit changes per transition, 
    104                                                  //              which prevents incorrect intermediate values during sampling.
    105                                                  // =========================================================================
    106                                                  
    107                                                  // Cross Read Pointer into Write Domain
    108             1                   20770868         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    109                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    110             1                        108                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    111             1                        108                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    112                                                      end else begin
    113             1                   20770760                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    114             1                   20770760                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    115                                                      end
    116                                                  end
    117                                              
    118                                                  // Cross Write Pointer into Read Domain
    119             1                   20772341         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    120                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    121             1                        110                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    122             1                        110                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    123                                                      end else begin
    124             1                   20772231                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    125             1                   20772231                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    126                                                      end
    127                                                  end
    128                                              
    129                                                  // =========================================================================
    130                                                  // 4. Status Flag Generation
    131                                                  // Description: Compares Gray-coded pointers to determine FIFO status.
    132                                                  //              - o_empty: True if read pointer equals synchronized write pointer.
    133                                                  //              - o_full : True if write pointer MSBs are inverted relative to 
    134                                                  //                         synchronized read pointer (indicating wrap-around).
    135                                                  // =========================================================================
    136             1                   15255038         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    137             1                   15247553         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);

    54                                                   // =========================================================================
    55                                                   // 1. Write Domain Logic
    56                                                   // Description: Updates the write pointer and its Gray-coded version.
    57                                                   //              Writes are only performed when 'i_wr_en' is active and FIFO 
    58                                                   //              is not full. Binary pointer is used for memory addressing.
    59                                                   // =========================================================================
    60              1                   12454051         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    61                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    62              1                        108                 wr_ptr <= 0;
    63              1                        108                 wr_ptr_gray <= 0;
    64                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    65              1                    6921732                 wr_ptr <= wr_ptr_next;
    66              1                    6921732                 wr_ptr_gray <= wr_ptr_next ^ (wr_ptr_next >> 1); 
    67                                                       end
    68                                                   end
    69                                               
    70                                                   // Synthesis of the Memory Array (Register-based)
    71              1                   12454051         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    72                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    73              1                        108                 for (j = 0; j < DEPTH; j = j + 1) begin
    73              2                       1728     
    74              1                       1728                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    75                                                           end
    76                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    77              1                    6921732                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    78                                                       end
    79                                                   end
    80                                               
    81                                                   // =========================================================================
    82                                                   // 2. Read Domain Logic
    83                                                   // Description: Updates the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    84                                                   //              Ensures safe data output only when FIFO is not empty.
    85                                                   //              Gray pointers are used to safely pass the pointer across domains.
    86                                                   // =========================================================================
    87              1                   13890973         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    88                                                       if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    89              1                        110                 rd_ptr <= 0;
    90              1                        110                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    91                                                       end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    92              1                    6921732                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;
    93              1                    6921732                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    94                                                       end
    95                                                   end
    96                                                   
    97                                                   // Data Output Assignment (zeroed if empty to prevent data leakage)
    98              1                    8409994         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    99                                               
    100                                                  // =========================================================================
    101                                                  // 3. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    102                                                  // Description: Synchronizes pointers between the two asynchronous domains.
    103                                                  //              Gray coding ensures only one bit changes per transition, 
    104                                                  //              which prevents incorrect intermediate values during sampling.
    105                                                  // =========================================================================
    106                                                  
    107                                                  // Cross Read Pointer into Write Domain
    108             1                   17984748         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    109                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    110             1                        108                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    111             1                        108                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    112                                                      end else begin
    113             1                   17984640                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    114             1                   17984640                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    115                                                      end
    116                                                  end
    117                                              
    118                                                  // Cross Write Pointer into Read Domain
    119             1                   17986211         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    120                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    121             1                        110                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    122             1                        110                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    123                                                      end else begin
    124             1                   17986101                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    125             1                   17986101                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    126                                                      end
    127                                                  end
    128                                              
    129                                                  // =========================================================================
    130                                                  // 4. Status Flag Generation
    131                                                  // Description: Compares Gray-coded pointers to determine FIFO status.
    132                                                  //              - o_empty: True if read pointer equals synchronized write pointer.
    133                                                  //              - o_full : True if write pointer MSBs are inverted relative to 
    134                                                  //                         synchronized read pointer (indicating wrap-around).
    135                                                  // =========================================================================
    136             1                   12456912         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    137             1                   12455427         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);

Statement Coverage for instance /tb_mipi_system_loopback/dut/u_csi2_rx_top/u_rx/u_fifo_l3 --

  File async_fifo_ppi_rx.v
    15                                               module async_fifo_ppi_rx #(
    16                                                   parameter WIDTH = 8,
    17                                                   parameter DEPTH = 16
    18                                               )(
    19                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    20                                                   // Write Domain Interface (Source: D-PHY Clock Domain)
    21                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    22                                                   input  wire              i_wr_clk,     // D-PHY Byte Clock
    23                                                   input  wire              i_wr_rst_n,   // Reset synchronized to wr_clk
    24                                                   input  wire              i_wr_en,      // Write enable
    25                                                   input  wire [WIDTH-1:0]  i_din,        // Data input from PPI
    26                                                   output wire              o_full,       // FIFO full flag
    27                                               
    28                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29                                                   // Read Domain Interface (Destination: System Clock Domain)
    30                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   input  wire              i_rd_clk,     // Internal System Clock
    32                                                   input  wire              i_rd_rst_n,   // Reset synchronized to rd_clk
    33                                                   input  wire              i_rd_en,      // Read enable from Lane Merger
    34                                                   output wire [WIDTH-1:0]  o_dout,       // Data output to Lane Merger
    35                                                   output wire              o_empty       // FIFO empty flag
    36                                               );
    37                                               
    38                                                   // Address width calculation
    39                                                   localparam ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH);
    40                                               
    41                                                   // Internal Memory and Pointer Registers
    42                                                   reg [WIDTH-1:0] mem [DEPTH-1:0];
    43                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr, rd_ptr;
    44                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray, rd_ptr_gray; 
    45                                                   
    46                                                   // Cross-Domain Synchronizers (2-Stage / Double-Flop)
    47                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray_sync1, wr_ptr_gray_sync2;
    48                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_gray_sync1, rd_ptr_gray_sync2;
    49                                               
    50                                                   // Pointer increments
    51              1                    4139626         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_next = wr_ptr + 1;
    52              1                    4139626         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_next = rd_ptr + 1;
    53                                                   integer j;
    54                                                   // =========================================================================
    55                                                   // 1. Write Domain Logic
    56                                                   // Description: Updates the write pointer and its Gray-coded version.
    57                                                   //              Writes are only performed when 'i_wr_en' is active and FIFO 
    58                                                   //              is not full. Binary pointer is used for memory addressing.
    59                                                   // =========================================================================
    60              1                    8279386         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    61                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    62              1                        108                 wr_ptr <= 0;
    63              1                        108                 wr_ptr_gray <= 0;
    64                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    65              1                    4139612                 wr_ptr <= wr_ptr_next;
    66              1                    4139612                 wr_ptr_gray <= wr_ptr_next ^ (wr_ptr_next >> 1); 
    67                                                       end
    68                                                   end
    69                                               
    70                                                   // Synthesis of the Memory Array (Register-based)
    71              1                    8279386         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    72                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    73              1                        108                 for (j = 0; j < DEPTH; j = j + 1) begin
    73              2                       1728     
    74              1                       1728                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    75                                                           end
    76                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    77              1                    4139612                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    78                                                       end
    79                                                   end
    80                                               
    81                                                   // =========================================================================
    82                                                   // 2. Read Domain Logic
    83                                                   // Description: Updates the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    84                                                   //              Ensures safe data output only when FIFO is not empty.
    85                                                   //              Gray pointers are used to safely pass the pointer across domains.
    86                                                   // =========================================================================
    87              1                    8325233         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    88                                                       if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    89              1                        110                 rd_ptr <= 0;
    90              1                        110                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    91                                                       end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    92              1                    4139612                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;
    93              1                    4139612                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    94                                                       end
    95                                                   end
    96                                                   
    97                                                   // Data Output Assignment (zeroed if empty to prevent data leakage)
    98              1                    4236219         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    99                                               
    100                                                  // =========================================================================
    101                                                  // 3. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    102                                                  // Description: Synchronizes pointers between the two asynchronous domains.
    103                                                  //              Gray coding ensures only one bit changes per transition, 
    104                                                  //              which prevents incorrect intermediate values during sampling.
    105                                                  // =========================================================================
    106                                                  
    107                                                  // Cross Read Pointer into Write Domain
    108             1                   12418998         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    109                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    110             1                        108                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    111             1                        108                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    112                                                      end else begin
    113             1                   12418890                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    114             1                   12418890                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    115                                                      end
    116                                                  end
    117                                              
    118                                                  // Cross Write Pointer into Read Domain
    119             1                   12419001         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    120                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    121             1                        110                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    122             1                        110                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    123                                                      end else begin
    124             1                   12418891                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    125             1                   12418891                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    126                                                      end
    127                                                  end
    128                                              
    129                                                  // =========================================================================
    130                                                  // 4. Status Flag Generation
    131                                                  // Description: Compares Gray-coded pointers to determine FIFO status.
    132                                                  //              - o_empty: True if read pointer equals synchronized write pointer.
    133                                                  //              - o_full : True if write pointer MSBs are inverted relative to 
    134                                                  //                         synchronized read pointer (indicating wrap-around).
    135                                                  // =========================================================================
    136             1                    8279238         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    137             1                    8279238         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);
