    32                                                   input  wire        i_RxSyncHS_0, i_RxSyncHS_1, i_RxSyncHS_2, i_RxSyncHS_3,
    33                                               
    34                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    35                                                   // MIPI D-PHY PPI RX Control & Error Signals (From PHY)
    36                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    37                                                   input  wire        i_Shutdown,
    38                                                   input  wire        i_Stopstate_0, i_Stopstate_1, i_Stopstate_2, i_Stopstate_3,
    39                                                   input  wire        i_RxUlpmEsc_0, i_RxUlpmEsc_1, i_RxUlpmEsc_2, i_RxUlpmEsc_3,
    40                                                   
    41                                                   input  wire        i_ErrSotHS_0, i_ErrSotHS_1, i_ErrSotHS_2, i_ErrSotHS_3,
    42                                                   input  wire        i_ErrSotSyncHS_0, i_ErrSotSyncHS_1, i_ErrSotSyncHS_2, i_ErrSotSyncHS_3,
    43                                                   input  wire        i_ErrControl_0, i_ErrControl_1, i_ErrControl_2, i_ErrControl_3,
    44                                                   input  wire        i_ErrEsc_0, i_ErrEsc_1, i_ErrEsc_2, i_ErrEsc_3,
    45                                               
    46                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    47                                                   // Internal Interface (To Controller / Lane Merger)
    48                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    49                                                   input  wire        i_lane0_rd_en, i_lane1_rd_en, i_lane2_rd_en, i_lane3_rd_en,
    50                                                   
    51                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_lane0_data,  o_lane1_data,  o_lane2_data,  o_lane3_data,
    52                                                   output wire        o_lane0_valid, o_lane1_valid, o_lane2_valid, o_lane3_valid,
    53                                                   
    54                                                   output wire        o_packet_active,
    55                                                   output wire        o_rx_ready,
    56                                                   output wire        o_rx_error_flag // Interrput to the System
    57                                               );
    58                                               
    59                                                   // =========================================================================
    60                                                   // CIL Error Handler Logic (100% Glitch-Free CDC)
    61                                                   // Concept: Aggregates multiple asynchronous error sources, captures them 
    62                                                   //          in the source domain, and safely crosses them using a 2-flop sync.
    63                                                   // =========================================================================
    64              1                          2         wire w_rx_error_comb = i_ErrSotHS_0 | i_ErrSotSyncHS_0 | i_ErrControl_0 | i_ErrEsc_0 |
    65                                                                          i_ErrSotHS_1 | i_ErrSotSyncHS_1 | i_ErrControl_1 | i_ErrEsc_1 |
    66                                                                          i_ErrSotHS_2 | i_ErrSotSyncHS_2 | i_ErrControl_2 | i_ErrEsc_2 |
    67                                                                          i_ErrSotHS_3 | i_ErrSotSyncHS_3 | i_ErrControl_3 | i_ErrEsc_3 ;
    68                                               
    69                                                   // Step 1: Register the combined error in the SOURCE domain (RX Clock) to prevent glitches
    70                                                   // Made it STICKY to prevent Data Loss in fast-to-slow crossing
    71                                                   reg r_rx_error_src_domain;
    72              1                        162         always @(posedge i_RxByteClkHS or negedge i_rst_n_rx) begin
    73                                                       if (!i_rst_n_rx) begin
    74              1                        108                 r_rx_error_src_domain <= 1'b0;
    75                                                       end else begin
    76                                                           // Sticky Error: Latch the error high so the System Clock NEVER misses it
    77              1                         54                 r_rx_error_src_domain <= r_rx_error_src_domain | w_rx_error_comb;
    78                                                       end
    79                                                   end
    80                                               
    81                                                   // Step 2: Synchronize the stable signal to the DESTINATION domain (System Clock)
    82                                                   reg [1:0] r_error_sync;
    83              1                        165         always @(posedge i_clk_sys or negedge i_rst_n_sys) begin
    84                                                       if (!i_rst_n_sys) begin
    85              1                        110                 r_error_sync <= 2'b00;
    86                                                       end else begin
    87              1                         55                 r_error_sync <= {r_error_sync[0], r_rx_error_src_domain};
    88                                                       end
    89                                                   end
    90                                                   assign o_rx_error_flag = r_error_sync[1]; // Safely synced to System Clock
    91                                               
    92                                                   // =========================================================================
    93                                                   // RX Data Path Logic
    94                                                   // Architecture: 4 Independent Asynchronous FIFOs to handle Clock Domain 
    95                                                   //               Crossing (CDC) for the payload data from the PHY clock 
    96                                                   //               domain to the core system clock domain.
    97                                                   // =========================================================================
    98              1                   22134650         wire w_wr_en_0 = i_RxValidHS_0 & i_RxActiveHS_0;
    99              1                   22128630         wire w_wr_en_1 = i_RxValidHS_1 & i_RxActiveHS_1;
    100             1                   16558450         wire w_wr_en_2 = i_RxValidHS_2 & i_RxActiveHS_2;
    101             1                   16558450         wire w_wr_en_3 = i_RxValidHS_3 & i_RxActiveHS_3;
    102                                              
    103                                                  wire w_empty_0, w_empty_1, w_empty_2, w_empty_3;
    104                                                  wire [7:0] w_raw_0, w_raw_1, w_raw_2, w_raw_3;
    105                                                  wire w_fifo_full_0, w_fifo_full_1, w_fifo_full_2, w_fifo_full_3;
    106                                              
    107                                                  // Safe read enable prevents underflow in the FIFOs
    108             1                   13944275         wire w_safe_rd_0 = i_lane0_rd_en & ~w_empty_0;
    109             1                   13941275         wire w_safe_rd_1 = i_lane1_rd_en & ~w_empty_1;
    110             1                   13938235         wire w_safe_rd_2 = i_lane2_rd_en & ~w_empty_2;
    111             1                   13938235         wire w_safe_rd_3 = i_lane3_rd_en & ~w_empty_3;
    112                                              
    113                                                  async_fifo_ppi_rx #(.WIDTH(8), .DEPTH(16)) u_fifo_l0 (
    114                                                      .i_wr_clk(i_RxByteClkHS), .i_wr_rst_n(i_rst_n_rx),
    115                                                      .i_rd_clk(i_clk_sys), .i_rd_rst_n(i_rst_n_sys),
    116                                                      .i_wr_en(w_wr_en_0), .i_din(i_RxDataHS_0), .i_rd_en(w_safe_rd_0),
    117                                                      .o_dout(w_raw_0), .o_empty(w_empty_0), .o_full(w_fifo_full_0)
    118                                                  );
    119                                              
    120                                                  async_fifo_ppi_rx #(.WIDTH(8), .DEPTH(16)) u_fifo_l1 (
    121                                                      .i_wr_clk(i_RxByteClkHS), .i_wr_rst_n(i_rst_n_rx),
    122                                                      .i_rd_clk(i_clk_sys), .i_rd_rst_n(i_rst_n_sys),
    123                                                      .i_wr_en(w_wr_en_1), .i_din(i_RxDataHS_1), .i_rd_en(w_safe_rd_1),
    124                                                      .o_dout(w_raw_1), .o_empty(w_empty_1), .o_full(w_fifo_full_1)
    125                                                  );
    126                                              
    127                                                  async_fifo_ppi_rx #(.WIDTH(8), .DEPTH(16)) u_fifo_l2 (
    128                                                      .i_wr_clk(i_RxByteClkHS), .i_wr_rst_n(i_rst_n_rx),
    129                                                      .i_rd_clk(i_clk_sys), .i_rd_rst_n(i_rst_n_sys),
    130                                                      .i_wr_en(w_wr_en_2), .i_din(i_RxDataHS_2), .i_rd_en(w_safe_rd_2),
    131                                                      .o_dout(w_raw_2), .o_empty(w_empty_2), .o_full(w_fifo_full_2)
    132                                                  );
    133                                              
    134                                                  async_fifo_ppi_rx #(.WIDTH(8), .DEPTH(16)) u_fifo_l3 (
    135                                                      .i_wr_clk(i_RxByteClkHS), .i_wr_rst_n(i_rst_n_rx),
    136                                                      .i_rd_clk(i_clk_sys), .i_rd_rst_n(i_rst_n_sys),
    137                                                      .i_wr_en(w_wr_en_3), .i_din(i_RxDataHS_3), .i_rd_en(w_safe_rd_3),
    138                                                      .o_dout(w_raw_3), .o_empty(w_empty_3), .o_full(w_fifo_full_3)
    139                                                  );
    140                                              
    141                                                  // =========================================================================
    142                                                  // Synthesis Optimization & Dummy Sink
    143                                                  // Concept: AND-reduction with 1'b0 creates a safe structural sink for 
    144                                                  //          unused interface signals, preventing synthesis tool warnings 
    145                                                  //          and ensuring clean connectivity.
    146                                                  // =========================================================================
    147             1                   11067328         wire w_dummy_sink = &{1'b0, i_RxSyncHS_0, i_RxSyncHS_1, i_RxSyncHS_2, i_RxSyncHS_3,
    148                                                                        w_fifo_full_0, w_fifo_full_1, w_fifo_full_2, w_fifo_full_3,
    149                                                                        i_Shutdown, i_Stopstate_0, i_Stopstate_1, i_Stopstate_2, i_Stopstate_3,
    150                                                                        i_RxUlpmEsc_0, i_RxUlpmEsc_1, i_RxUlpmEsc_2, i_RxUlpmEsc_3};
    151                                              
    152                                                  // The dummy sink is logically OR'ed with o_rx_ready (X | 0 = X)
    153             1                      97811         assign o_rx_ready = (~w_empty_0) | w_dummy_sink; 
    154                                              
    155                                                  // Output assignment & gating
    156             1                   13944275         assign o_lane0_valid = ~w_empty_0 & i_lane0_rd_en;
    157             1                   13941275         assign o_lane1_valid = ~w_empty_1 & i_lane1_rd_en;
    158             1                   13938235         assign o_lane2_valid = ~w_empty_2 & i_lane2_rd_en;
    159             1                   13938235         assign o_lane3_valid = ~w_empty_3 & i_lane3_rd_en;
    160                                              
    161             1                   23562421         assign o_lane0_data = o_lane0_valid ? w_raw_0 : 8'h00;
    162             1                   20785653         assign o_lane1_data = o_lane1_valid ? w_raw_1 : 8'h00;
    163             1                   12448108         assign o_lane2_data = o_lane2_valid ? w_raw_2 : 8'h00;
    164             1                   12448324         assign o_lane3_data = o_lane3_valid ? w_raw_3 : 8'h00;
    165                                              
    166                                                  // =========================================================================
    167                                                  // Packet Active State Machine (Hang Timer)
    168                                                  // Concept: Maintains the active state of the packet for a set duration 
    169                                                  //          (15 cycles) after the FIFO empties. This acts as a tolerance 
    170                                                  //          buffer to prevent premature packet termination during inter-lane 
    171                                                  //          skew or brief interruptions.
    172                                                  // =========================================================================
    173                                                  reg [3:0] rx_hang_timer;
    174                                                  reg r_packet_active;
    175                                                  
    176             1                     276881         always @(posedge i_clk_sys or negedge i_rst_n_sys) begin
    177                                                      if (!i_rst_n_sys) begin
    178             1                        110                 r_packet_active <= 1'b0;
    179             1                        110                 rx_hang_timer   <= 4'd0;
    180                                                      end else begin
    181                                                          if (~w_empty_0) begin 
    182             1                      97768                     r_packet_active <= 1'b1;
    183             1                      97768                     rx_hang_timer   <= 4'd15; 
    184                                                          end else if (rx_hang_timer > 0) begin
    185             1                     168144                     rx_hang_timer   <= rx_hang_timer - 1;
    186                                                          end else begin
    187             1                      10859                     r_packet_active <= 1'b0;

Statement Coverage for instance /tb_mipi_system_loopback/dut/u_csi2_rx_top/u_desc0 --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File mipi_descrambler.v
    14                                               module mipi_descrambler #(
    15                                                   parameter [15:0] LANE_SEED = 16'h0810 
    16                                               )(
    17                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18                                                   // System Clock & Reset
    19                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    20                                                   input  wire        i_clk,
    21                                                   input  wire        i_rst_n,
    22                                                   
    23                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24                                                   // Control & Input Data Stream
    25                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    26                                                   input  wire        i_packet_active,  // Resets LFSR to SEED when de-asserted
    27                                                   input  wire [7:0]  i_scrambled_data, 
    28                                                   input  wire        i_lane_vld,       // Data valid for the current lane
    29                                                   
    30                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   // Descrambled Data Outputs
    32                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    33                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_descrambled_data,
    34                                                   output wire        o_lane_vld_out
    35                                               );
    36                                               
    37                                                   // LFSR State Register
    38                                                   reg  [15:0] r_lfsr_state;
    39                                                   
    40                                                   // Intermediate wires for Parallel Logic evaluation
    41                                                   wire [7:0]  w_key_stream;
    42                                                   wire [7:0]  w_fb;
    43                                                   wire [15:0] w_lfsr_next;
    44                                               
    45                                                   // =========================================================================
    46                                                   // 1. Hardware Unrolling (Parallel Feedback Evaluation)
    47                                                   // Description: Calculates 8 bits of LFSR feedback in a single cycle using 
    48                                                   //              Combinational Logic. This eliminates the need for 'for-loops' 
    49                                                   //              and avoids multiple driver issues during synthesis.
    50                                                   // Polynomial: Evaluates the specific MIPI-standard scrambling taps.
    51                                                   // =========================================================================
    52              1                    9710256         assign w_fb[0] = r_lfsr_state[0] ^ r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5];
    53              1                    9710256         assign w_fb[1] = r_lfsr_state[1] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6];
    54              1                    9710256         assign w_fb[2] = r_lfsr_state[2] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7];
    55              1                    9710256         assign w_fb[3] = r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8];
    56              1                    9710256         assign w_fb[4] = r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9];
    57              1                    9710256         assign w_fb[5] = r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10];
    58              1                    9710256         assign w_fb[6] = r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11];
    59              1                    9710256         assign w_fb[7] = r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11] ^ r_lfsr_state[12];
    60                                               
    61                                                   // The key-stream is the lowest 8 bits of the current state
    62              1                    9710256         assign w_key_stream = r_lfsr_state[7:0];
    63                                               
    64                                                   // Construct the next 16-bit state by shifting and inserting feedback bits
    65              1                   19366301         assign w_lfsr_next  = {w_fb[7], w_fb[6], w_fb[5], w_fb[4], w_fb[3], w_fb[2], w_fb[1], w_fb[0], r_lfsr_state[15:8]};
    66                                               
    67                                                   // =========================================================================
    68                                                   // 2. LFSR State Controller
    69                                                   // Description: Manages initialization and state transitions.
    70                                                   //              - Reset or Idle: Re-initializes state to the 'LANE_SEED'.
    71                                                   //              - Active Reception: Advances the LFSR state per valid byte.
    72                                                   // =========================================================================
    73              1                   16644455         always @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin
    74              1                        110             if (!i_rst_n)                 r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    75              1                      10859             else if (!i_packet_active)    r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    76              1                    9704852             else if (i_lane_vld)          r_lfsr_state <= w_lfsr_next;
    77                                                   end
    78                                               
    79                                                   // =========================================================================
    80                                                   // 3. Descrambling Operation (XOR)
    81                                                   // Description: Recovers the original data by XORing the scrambled input 
    82                                                   //              with the generated key-stream. Output is gated by 'i_lane_vld'.
    83                                                   // =========================================================================
    84              1                   46913646         assign o_descrambled_data = i_lane_vld ? (i_scrambled_data ^ w_key_stream) : 8'h00;

    Line         Item                      Count     Source

  File mipi_descrambler.v
    14                                               module mipi_descrambler #(
    15                                                   parameter [15:0] LANE_SEED = 16'h0810 
    16                                               )(
    17                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18                                                   // System Clock & Reset
    19                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    20                                                   input  wire        i_clk,
    21                                                   input  wire        i_rst_n,
    22                                                   
    23                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24                                                   // Control & Input Data Stream
    25                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    26                                                   input  wire        i_packet_active,  // Resets LFSR to SEED when de-asserted
    27                                                   input  wire [7:0]  i_scrambled_data, 
    28                                                   input  wire        i_lane_vld,       // Data valid for the current lane
    29                                                   
    30                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   // Descrambled Data Outputs
    32                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    33                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_descrambled_data,
    34                                                   output wire        o_lane_vld_out
    35                                               );
    36                                               
    37                                                   // LFSR State Register
    38                                                   reg  [15:0] r_lfsr_state;
    39                                                   
    40                                                   // Intermediate wires for Parallel Logic evaluation
    41                                                   wire [7:0]  w_key_stream;
    42                                                   wire [7:0]  w_fb;
    43                                                   wire [15:0] w_lfsr_next;
    44                                               
    45                                                   // =========================================================================
    46                                                   // 1. Hardware Unrolling (Parallel Feedback Evaluation)
    47                                                   // Description: Calculates 8 bits of LFSR feedback in a single cycle using 
    48                                                   //              Combinational Logic. This eliminates the need for 'for-loops' 
    49                                                   //              and avoids multiple driver issues during synthesis.
    50                                                   // Polynomial: Evaluates the specific MIPI-standard scrambling taps.
    51                                                   // =========================================================================
    52              1                    6925636         assign w_fb[0] = r_lfsr_state[0] ^ r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5];
    53              1                    6925636         assign w_fb[1] = r_lfsr_state[1] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6];
    54              1                    6925636         assign w_fb[2] = r_lfsr_state[2] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7];
    55              1                    6925636         assign w_fb[3] = r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8];
    56              1                    6925636         assign w_fb[4] = r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9];
    57              1                    6925636         assign w_fb[5] = r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10];
    58              1                    6925636         assign w_fb[6] = r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11];
    59              1                    6925636         assign w_fb[7] = r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11] ^ r_lfsr_state[12];
    60                                               
    61                                                   // The key-stream is the lowest 8 bits of the current state
    62              1                    6925636         assign w_key_stream = r_lfsr_state[7:0];
    63                                               
    64                                                   // Construct the next 16-bit state by shifting and inserting feedback bits
    65              1                   13836675         assign w_lfsr_next  = {w_fb[7], w_fb[6], w_fb[5], w_fb[4], w_fb[3], w_fb[2], w_fb[1], w_fb[0], r_lfsr_state[15:8]};
    66                                               
    67                                                   // =========================================================================
    68                                                   // 2. LFSR State Controller
    69                                                   // Description: Manages initialization and state transitions.
    70                                                   //              - Reset or Idle: Re-initializes state to the 'LANE_SEED'.
    71                                                   //              - Active Reception: Advances the LFSR state per valid byte.
    72                                                   // =========================================================================
    73              1                   13858335         always @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin
    74              1                        110             if (!i_rst_n)                 r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    75              1                       9359             else if (!i_packet_active)    r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    76              1                    6921732             else if (i_lane_vld)          r_lfsr_state <= w_lfsr_next;
    77                                                   end
    78                                               
    79                                                   // =========================================================================
    80                                                   // 3. Descrambling Operation (XOR)
    81                                                   // Description: Recovers the original data by XORing the scrambled input 
    82                                                   //              with the generated key-stream. Output is gated by 'i_lane_vld'.
    83                                                   // =========================================================================
    84              1                   41392422         assign o_descrambled_data = i_lane_vld ? (i_scrambled_data ^ w_key_stream) : 8'h00;

Toggle Coverage:

Statement Coverage for instance /tb_mipi_system_loopback/dut/u_csi2_rx_top/u_desc3 --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File mipi_descrambler.v
    14                                               module mipi_descrambler #(
    15                                                   parameter [15:0] LANE_SEED = 16'h0810 
    16                                               )(
    17                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18                                                   // System Clock & Reset
    19                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    20                                                   input  wire        i_clk,
    21                                                   input  wire        i_rst_n,
    22                                                   
    23                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24                                                   // Control & Input Data Stream
    25                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    26                                                   input  wire        i_packet_active,  // Resets LFSR to SEED when de-asserted
    27                                                   input  wire [7:0]  i_scrambled_data, 
    28                                                   input  wire        i_lane_vld,       // Data valid for the current lane
    29                                                   
    30                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   // Descrambled Data Outputs
    32                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    33                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_descrambled_data,
    34                                                   output wire        o_lane_vld_out
    35                                               );
    36                                               
    37                                                   // LFSR State Register
    38                                                   reg  [15:0] r_lfsr_state;
    39                                                   
    40                                                   // Intermediate wires for Parallel Logic evaluation
    41                                                   wire [7:0]  w_key_stream;
    42                                                   wire [7:0]  w_fb;
    43                                                   wire [15:0] w_lfsr_next;
    44                                               
    45                                                   // =========================================================================
    46                                                   // 1. Hardware Unrolling (Parallel Feedback Evaluation)
    47                                                   // Description: Calculates 8 bits of LFSR feedback in a single cycle using 
    48                                                   //              Combinational Logic. This eliminates the need for 'for-loops' 
    49                                                   //              and avoids multiple driver issues during synthesis.
    50                                                   // Polynomial: Evaluates the specific MIPI-standard scrambling taps.
    51                                                   // =========================================================================
    52              1                    4142016         assign w_fb[0] = r_lfsr_state[0] ^ r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5];
    53              1                    4142016         assign w_fb[1] = r_lfsr_state[1] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6];
    54              1                    4142016         assign w_fb[2] = r_lfsr_state[2] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7];
    55              1                    4142016         assign w_fb[3] = r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8];
    56              1                    4142016         assign w_fb[4] = r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9];
    57              1                    4142016         assign w_fb[5] = r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10];
    58              1                    4142016         assign w_fb[6] = r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11];
    59              1                    4142016         assign w_fb[7] = r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11] ^ r_lfsr_state[12];
    60                                               
    61                                                   // The key-stream is the lowest 8 bits of the current state
    62              1                    4142016         assign w_key_stream = r_lfsr_state[7:0];
    63                                               
    64                                                   // Construct the next 16-bit state by shifting and inserting feedback bits
    65              1                    8269119         assign w_lfsr_next  = {w_fb[7], w_fb[6], w_fb[5], w_fb[4], w_fb[3], w_fb[2], w_fb[1], w_fb[0], r_lfsr_state[15:8]};
    66                                               
    67                                                   // =========================================================================
    68                                                   // 2. LFSR State Controller
    69                                                   // Description: Manages initialization and state transitions.
    70                                                   //              - Reset or Idle: Re-initializes state to the 'LANE_SEED'.
    71                                                   //              - Active Reception: Advances the LFSR state per valid byte.
    72                                                   // =========================================================================
    73              1                    8292595         always @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin
    74              1                        110             if (!i_rst_n)                 r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    75              1                       7859             else if (!i_packet_active)    r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    76              1                    4139612             else if (i_lane_vld)          r_lfsr_state <= w_lfsr_next;
    77                                                   end
    78                                               
    79                                                   // =========================================================================
    80                                                   // 3. Descrambling Operation (XOR)
    81                                                   // Description: Recovers the original data by XORing the scrambled input 
    82                                                   //              with the generated key-stream. Output is gated by 'i_lane_vld'.
    83                                                   // =========================================================================
    84              1                   24731303         assign o_descrambled_data = i_lane_vld ? (i_scrambled_data ^ w_key_stream) : 8'h00;

    13                                               module lane_merger (
    14                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15                                                   // System Clock & Reset
    16                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    17                                                   input  wire        i_clk,
    18                                                   input  wire        i_rst_n,
    19                                                   
    20                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21                                                   // Configuration & Control Signals
    22                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    23                                                   input  wire [2:0]  i_active_lanes, 
    24                                                   input  wire        i_packet_active, 
    25                                                   input  wire        i_rx_ready,     
    26                                               
    27                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28                                                   // Parallel Data Inputs (From PPI RX FIFOs)
    29                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    30                                                   input  wire [7:0]  i_lane0_data, i_lane1_data, i_lane2_data, i_lane3_data,
    31                                                   input  wire        i_lane0_vld,  i_lane1_vld,  i_lane2_vld,  i_lane3_vld, 
    32                                               
    33                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34                                                   // Serial Data Outputs & FIFO Pull Control
    35                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    36                                                   output wire        o_pull_data,
    37                                                   output reg  [7:0]  o_merged_data,
    38                                                   output reg         o_merged_valid
    39                                               );
    40                                               
    41                                                   // Internal Buffers & FSM Registers
    42                                                   reg [7:0] r_buf1, r_buf2, r_buf3;
    43                                                   reg [3:1] r_vld_buf; 
    44                                                   reg [1:0] r_count;
    45                                                   reg [1:0] next_count; 
    46                                                   
    47                                                   reg [2:0] startup_delay;
    48                                                   reg       r_is_active;
    49                                               
    50                                                   // =========================================================================
    51                                                   // 1. Startup Delay & Active State Controller
    52                                                   // Description: Introduces a brief initialization delay (3 cycles) upon 
    53                                                   //              receiving 'i_rx_ready' before starting to pull data. This 
    54                                                   //              ensures data alignment and prevents premature reads.
    55                                                   // =========================================================================
    56              1                   22167589         always @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin
    57                                                       if (!i_rst_n) begin
    58              1                        110                 startup_delay <= 3'd3;
    59              1                        110                 r_is_active   <= 1'b0;
    60                                                       end else if (!i_packet_active && (r_count == 2'd0) && !o_pull_data) begin
    61              1                      16261                 startup_delay <= 3'd3;
    62              1                      16261                 r_is_active   <= 1'b0;
    63                                                       end else if (i_rx_ready && !r_is_active) begin
    64              1                      16206                 if (startup_delay > 0) startup_delay <= startup_delay - 1;
    65              1                       5402                 else                   r_is_active <= 1'b1;
    66                                                       end
    67                                                   end
    68                                               
    69                                                   // Pull condition: Only assert read enable when active, in FSM state 0, and RX is ready
    70              1                   22140416         assign o_pull_data = r_is_active && (r_count == 2'd0) && i_rx_ready;
    71                                                   
    72                                                   // =========================================================================
    73                                                   // 2. Output Multiplexer FSM (Next State Logic)
    74                                                   // Description: Determines the sequence of outputting lanes based on the 
    75                                                   //              active lane configuration (1, 2, or 4 lanes). Loops back 
    76                                                   //              to state 0 to fetch the next parallel word.
    77                                                   // =========================================================================
    78              1                   22129657         always @(*) begin
    79              1                   22129657             next_count = r_count;
    80                                                       case (r_count)
    81              1                    6921732                 2'd0: if (o_pull_data && i_active_lanes > 1) next_count = 2'd1;
    82              1                    4139612                 2'd1: if (i_active_lanes > 2) next_count = 2'd2; else next_count = 2'd0;
    82              2                    2782120     
    83              1                    4139612                 2'd2: if (i_active_lanes > 3) next_count = 2'd3; else next_count = 2'd0;
    83              2                    ***0***     
    84              1                    4139612                 2'd3: next_count = 2'd0;
    85              1                          1                 default: next_count = 2'd0;
    86                                                       endcase
    87                                                   end
    88                                               
    89                                                   // =========================================================================
    90                                                   // 3. Datapath & Pipelined Buffering
    91                                                   // Description: 
    92                                                   //   - State 0: Outputs Lane 0 directly and concurrently buffers Lanes 1/2/3.
    93                                                   //   - State 1/2/3: Streams the buffered data sequentially out to the MAC.
    94                                                   //   This ensures zero dead-cycles when converting parallel to serial.
    95                                                   // =========================================================================
    96              1                   24927621         always @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin
    97                                                       if (!i_rst_n) begin
    98              1                        110                 r_count        <= 2'd0;
    99              1                        110                 o_merged_data  <= 8'h00; 
    100             1                        110                 o_merged_valid <= 1'b0;
    101             1                        110                 r_buf1 <= 8'h00; r_buf2 <= 8'h00; r_buf3 <= 8'h00;
    101             2                        110     
    101             3                        110     
    102             1                        110                 r_vld_buf <= 3'b000;
    103                                                      end else if (!i_packet_active && (r_count == 2'd0) && !o_pull_data) begin
    104             1                       5457                 r_count        <= 2'd0;
    105             1                       5457                 o_merged_data  <= 8'h00; 
    106             1                       5457                 o_merged_valid <= 1'b0;
    107                                                      end else begin
    108             1                   24922054                 r_count <= next_count;
    109                                                          case (r_count)
    110                                                              2'd0: begin
    111                                                                  if (o_pull_data) begin
    112                                                                      // Directly output Lane 0
    113             1                    9704852                             o_merged_data  <= i_lane0_vld ? i_lane0_data : 8'h00;
    114             1                    9704852                             o_merged_valid <= i_lane0_vld; 
    115                                                                      
    116                                                                      // Buffer remaining lanes for upcoming cycles
    117             1                    9704852                             r_buf1       <= i_lane1_data; 
    118             1                    9704852                             r_vld_buf[1] <= i_lane1_vld;
    119             1                    9704852                             r_buf2       <= i_lane2_data; 
    120             1                    9704852                             r_vld_buf[2] <= i_lane2_vld;
    121             1                    9704852                             r_buf3       <= i_lane3_data; 
    122             1                    9704852                             r_vld_buf[3] <= i_lane3_vld;
    123                                                                  end else begin

================================Statement Details================================

Statement Coverage for instance /tb_mipi_system_loopback/dut/u_csi2_rx_top/u_depacketizer --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File depacketizer.v
    3                                                module rx_depacketizer (
    4                                                    input  wire         i_clk,
    5                                                    input  wire         i_rst_n,
    6                                                    
    7                                                    // Configuration
    8                                                    input  wire         i_cfg_crc_en,   // <-- ADDED: CRC Enable Input
    9                                                    
    10                                                   // Input Stream
    11                                                   input  wire [7:0]   i_rx_data,
    12                                                   input  wire         i_rx_valid,
    13                                                   
    14                                                   // Output to Unpacker (Payload)
    15                                                   output reg  [7:0]   o_pay_data,
    16                                                   output reg          o_pay_valid,
    17                                                   output reg          o_pay_last,
    18                                                   output reg          o_pay_user,
    19                                                   
    20                                                   // Output to External ECC (Header)
    21                                                   output reg  [31:0]  o_ecc_hdr_data,
    22                                                   // Pulses HIGH for exactly 1 cycle when the 4th header byte is registered
    23                                                   // and o_ecc_hdr_data is valid. Drives i_header_valid of csi2_ecc_rx.
    24                                                   output reg          o_hdr_captured,
    25                                               
    26                                                   // ECC-corrected header feedback (registered output of csi2_ecc_rx,
    27                                                   // arrives exactly 1 cycle after o_hdr_captured pulses).
    28                                                   // Layout: {2'b00, WC[15:0], DI[7:0]}
    29                                                   input  wire [25:0]  i_ecc_corrected_header,
    30                                                   input  wire         i_ecc_header_valid,
    31                                                   // HIGH for 1 cycle (same cycle as i_ecc_header_valid would be, but on
    32                                                   // double-bit errors i_ecc_header_valid is suppressed and only this fires).
    33                                                   // The depacketizer uses it to abort and flush the corrupted packet.
    34                                                   input  wire         i_ecc_double_err,
    35                                               
    36                                                   // Output to External CRC (Now Optional based on i_cfg_crc_en)
    37                                                   output reg  [7:0]   o_crc_data,
    38                                                   output reg          o_crc_valid,
    39                                                   output reg          o_crc_start,
    40                                                   output reg          o_crc_end
    41                                               );
    42                                               
    43                                                   // =========================================================================
    44                                                   // FSM State Definitions
    45                                                   // =========================================================================
    46                                                   localparam ST_HEADER  = 2'd0;
    47                                                   localparam ST_PAYLOAD = 2'd1;
    48                                                   localparam ST_FOOTER  = 2'd2;
    49                                               
    50                                                   reg [1:0] state, next_state;
    51                                               
    52                                                   // =========================================================================
    53                                                   // Internal Registers & Counters
    54                                                   // =========================================================================
    55                                                   reg [1:0]  hdr_cnt;       
    56                                                   reg [15:0] pay_cnt;       
    57                                                   reg        ftr_cnt;       
    58                                                   
    59                                                   reg [31:0] hdr_shift_reg; 
    60                                                   reg [15:0] word_count;
    61                                                   // word_count is loaded from raw header bytes the cycle hdr_cnt==3,
    62                                                   // then overridden by i_ecc_corrected_header exactly 1 cycle later
    63                                                   // (when pay_cnt==0 in ST_PAYLOAD). The exit comparison pay_cnt==word_count-1
    64                                                   // is only reached at pay_cnt=WC-1, so the correction at pay_cnt=0 is
    65                                                   // always in time for any realistic line length (WC >= 4).
    66                                               
    67                                                   // =========================================================================
    68                                                   // FSM Sequential Logic
    69                                                   // =========================================================================
    70                                                  reg frame_started; // <-- ADD THIS FLAG
    71                                               
    72              1                    9710594         always @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin
    73                                                       if (!i_rst_n) begin
    74              1                        218                 state          <= ST_HEADER;
    75              1                        218                 hdr_cnt        <= 2'd0;
    76              1                        218                 pay_cnt        <= 16'd0;
    77              1                        218                 ftr_cnt        <= 1'b0;
    78              1                        218                 hdr_shift_reg  <= 32'd0;
    79              1                        218                 word_count     <= 16'd0;
    80              1                        218                 o_ecc_hdr_data <= 32'd0;
    81              1                        218                 o_hdr_captured <= 1'b0;
    82              1                        218                 frame_started  <= 1'b0;
    83                                                       end else begin
    84              1                    9710376                 state          <= next_state;
    85              1                    9710376                 o_hdr_captured <= 1'b0; // default: pulse for 1 cycle only
    86                                               
    87                                                           // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    88                                                           // ECC word_count override.
    89                                                           // i_ecc_header_valid arrives exactly 1 cycle after o_hdr_captured.
    90                                                           // At that point the FSM has just entered ST_PAYLOAD (pay_cnt==0).
    91                                                           // Overwriting word_count here is safe: the exit condition
    92                                                           // (pay_cnt == word_count-1) is only checked at pay_cnt = WC-1,
    93                                                           // which is at least WC-1 cycles away. For all line lengths >= 2
    94                                                           // the corrected value is committed before the exit is ever evaluated.
    95                                                           // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    96                                                           if (i_ecc_header_valid) begin
    97              1                       5401                     word_count <= i_ecc_corrected_header[23:8]; // WC[15:0]
    98                                                           end
    99                                               
    100                                                          // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    101                                                          // Double-bit error: the received WC is uncorrectable garbage.
    102                                                          // Abort the current packet immediately by forcing the FSM back to
    103                                                          // ST_HEADER. This drops the corrupted packet payload and re-syncs
    104                                                          // the byte stream at the next packet header boundary.
    105                                                          // Without this, a corrupted word_count locks the FSM in ST_PAYLOAD
    106                                                          // for the wrong number of bytes, misaligning all subsequent packets.
    107                                                          // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    108                                                          if (i_ecc_double_err) begin
    109             1                        995                     state      <= ST_HEADER;
    110             1                        995                     hdr_cnt    <= 2'd0;
    111             1                        995                     pay_cnt    <= 16'd0;
    112             1                        995                     ftr_cnt    <= 1'b0;
    113                                                          end else if (i_rx_valid) begin
    114                                                              case (state)
    115                                                                  ST_HEADER: begin
    116             1                      22048                             hdr_shift_reg[hdr_cnt*8 +: 8] <= i_rx_data;
    117                                                                      if (hdr_cnt == 2'd3) begin
    118             1                       5512                                 hdr_cnt        <= 2'd0;
    119             1                       5512                                 o_ecc_hdr_data <= {i_rx_data, hdr_shift_reg[23:0]};
    120                                                                          // Load raw word_count immediately so the FSM can
    121                                                                          // transition to ST_PAYLOAD without stalling.
    122                                                                          // ECC will override this 1 cycle later if needed.
    123             1                       5512                                 word_count     <= {hdr_shift_reg[23:16], hdr_shift_reg[15:8]};
    124                                                                          // Pulse the ECC trigger ONLY for long packets.
    125                                                                          // Short packets (DT[5:4]==2'b00: Frame Start/End,
    126                                                                          // Line Start/End) carry a frame/line number in the
    127                                                                          // WC field bytes, not a byte count.  If the ECC
    128                                                                          // checker were invoked on them, two problems arise:
    129                                                                          //  1. The TX ECC was computed from cfg_wc (long-pkt),
    130                                                                          //     so the RX sees a false syndrome on every
    131                                                                          //     Frame Start / Frame End -> spurious ECC flag.
    132                                                                          //  2. The ECC feedback would overwrite word_count
    133                                                                          //     with the frame/line number, corrupting the
    134                                                                          //     payload length of the next long packet.
    135                                                                          // Suppressing o_hdr_captured for short packets fixes
    136                                                                          // both problems without any other RTL changes.
    137                                                                          if (hdr_shift_reg[5:4] != 2'b00)
    138             1                       5402                                     o_hdr_captured <= 1'b1;
    139                                              
    140                                                                          if (hdr_shift_reg[5:0] == 6'h00)
    141             1                         56                                     frame_started <= 1'b1;
    142                                                                      end else begin
    143             1                      16536                                 hdr_cnt <= hdr_cnt + 1'b1;
    144                                                                      end
    145                                                                  end
    146                                                                  ST_PAYLOAD: begin
    147             1                       5402                             if (pay_cnt == 16'd0) frame_started <= 1'b0;
    148                                              
    149             1                       5401                             if (pay_cnt == word_count - 1'b1) pay_cnt <= 16'd0;
    150             1                    9670600                             else                              pay_cnt <= pay_cnt + 1'b1;
    151                                                                  end
    152                                                                  ST_FOOTER: begin
    153             1                       2901                             if (ftr_cnt == 1'b1) ftr_cnt <= 1'b0;
    154             1                       2901                             else                 ftr_cnt <= ftr_cnt + 1'b1;
    155                                                                  end
    156                                                              endcase
    157                                                          end
    158                                                      end
    159                                                  end
    160                                              
    161                                                  // =========================================================================
    162                                                  // FSM Next State Logic
    163                                                  // =========================================================================
    164             1                    9714754         always @* begin
    165             1                    9714754             next_state = state;
    166                                                      // Double-bit error abort overrides everything: go straight to ST_HEADER.
    167                                                      if (i_ecc_double_err) begin
    168             1                          3                 next_state = ST_HEADER;
    169                                                      end else if (i_rx_valid) begin
    170                                                          case (state)
    171                                                              ST_HEADER: begin
    172                                                                  if (hdr_cnt == 2'd3) begin
    173                                                                      // Check Data Type in DI byte (hdr_shift_reg[7:0])
    174                                                                      // MIPI Data Types 0x00 to 0x0F are Short Packets
    175                                                                      if (hdr_shift_reg[5:4] == 2'b00) begin
    176             1                        110                                 next_state = ST_HEADER; // Skip Payload and Footer entirely
    177                                                                      end 
    178                                                                      // Safeguard: Long packet but with 0-byte payload
    179                                                                      else if ({hdr_shift_reg[23:16], hdr_shift_reg[15:8]} == 16'd0) begin
    180             1                    ***0***                                 if (i_cfg_crc_en) next_state = ST_FOOTER;
    181             1                    ***0***                                 else              next_state = ST_HEADER;
    182                                                                      end 
    183                                                                      // Normal Long Packet
    184                                                                      else begin
    185             1                       5402                                 next_state = ST_PAYLOAD;
    186                                                                      end
    187                                                                  end
    188                                                              end
    189                                                              ST_PAYLOAD: begin
    190                                                                  if (pay_cnt == word_count - 1'b1) begin
    191             1                       2901                             if (i_cfg_crc_en) next_state = ST_FOOTER;
    192             1                       2500                             else              next_state = ST_HEADER;
    193                                                                  end
    194                                                              end
    195                                                              ST_FOOTER: begin 
    196             1                       2901                         if (ftr_cnt == 1'b1) next_state = ST_HEADER;
    197                                                              end
    198                                                          endcase
    199                                                      end
    200                                                  end
    201                                              
    202                                                  // =========================================================================
    203                                                  // Output Combinational Logic
    204                                                  // =========================================================================
    205             1                   19356364         always @* begin
    206                                                      // Defaults to prevent latches
    207             1                   19356364             o_pay_data  = i_rx_data;
    208             1                   19356364             o_pay_valid = 1'b0;
    209             1                   19356364             o_pay_last  = 1'b0;
    210             1                   19356364             o_pay_user  = 1'b0;
    211                                              
    212             1                   19356364             o_crc_data  = i_rx_data;
    213             1                   19356364             o_crc_valid = 1'b0;
    214             1                   19356364             o_crc_start = 1'b0;
    215             1                   19356364             o_crc_end   = 1'b0;
    216                                              
    217                                                      if (i_rx_valid) begin
    218                                                          if (state == ST_PAYLOAD) begin
    219             1                   19313871                     o_pay_valid = 1'b1;
    220             1                   19313871                     o_pay_user  = (pay_cnt == 16'd0) && frame_started; 
    221             1                   19313871                     o_pay_last  = (pay_cnt == word_count - 1'b1);
    222                                              
    223             1                   19313871                     o_crc_valid = 1'b1;
    224             1                   19313871                     o_crc_start = (pay_cnt == 16'd0);
    225             1                   19313871                     o_crc_end   = (pay_cnt == word_count - 1'b1); 
    226                                                          end
    227                                                      end
    228                                              
    229                                                      // <-- THE UN-STICK FIX -->
    230                                                      // Force the CRC valid signal HIGH during the entire footer, even if the PHY 
    231                                                      // drops i_rx_valid early. This guarantees the crc_rx FSM gets the clock 
    232                                                      // cycles it needs to reach State 2 and pulse o_crc_done!
    233                                                      if (state == ST_FOOTER) begin
    234             1                       8681                 o_crc_valid = 1'b1;
